1	"""``minima config`` — the pre-TUI credential setup command.
2	
3	Sectioned guided setup plus non-interactive ``list``/``get``/``set``/``unset``/``doctor``/
4	``path``. Secrets are never echoed: interactive entry uses ``getpass``, and ``list``/``get``
5	mask values to the last 4 characters. Backed by :mod:`minima_harness.tui.config_store`.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import getpass
11	import os
12	import sys
13	
14	from minima_harness.minima.config import DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL
15	from minima_harness.tui import config_store as store
16	
17	_USAGE = (
18	    "usage: minima config "
19	    "[list | get <KEY> | set <KEY> <VALUE> | unset <KEY> | doctor | path]\n"
20	    "       minima config            # interactive guided setup"
21	)
22	
23	
24	def _list() -> int:
25	    print(f"config file:     {store.CONFIG_FILE}")
26	    print(f"secrets backend: {store.backend_name()} (service '{store.KEYRING_SERVICE}')\n")
27	    for section in store.SECTIONS:
28	        print(f"[{section.title}]")
29	        for f in section.fields:
30	            val = store.get(f.key)
31	            if val:
32	                shown = store.mask(val) if f.secret else val
33	                print(f"  {f.key:<20} {shown:<26} ({store.location(f.key)})")
34	            else:
35	                print(f"  {f.key:<20} {'—':<26} ({'optional' if f.optional else 'MISSING'})")
36	        print()
37	    return 0
38	
39	
40	def _interactive() -> int:
41	    print("minima config — press Enter to keep the current value.\n")
42	    for section in store.SECTIONS:
43	        print(f"# {section.title} — {section.note}")
44	        for f in section.fields:
45	            cur = store.get(f.key)
46	            if f.secret:
47	                shown = store.mask(cur) if cur else "unset"

"""``minima config`` — the pre-TUI credential setup command.

Sectioned guided setup plus non-interactive ``list``/``get``/``set``/``unset``/``doctor``/
``path``. Secrets are never echoed: interactive entry uses ``getpass``, and ``list``/``get``
mask values to the last 4 characters. Backed by :mod:`minima_harness.tui.config_store`.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import getpass
import os
import sys

from minima_harness.minima.config import DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL
from minima_harness.tui import config_store as store

_USAGE = (
    "usage: minima config "
    "[list | get <KEY> | set <KEY> <VALUE> | unset <KEY> | doctor | path]\n"
    "       minima config            # interactive guided setup"
)


def _list() -> int:
    print(f"config file:     {store.CONFIG_FILE}")
    print(f"secrets backend: {store.backend_name()} (service '{store.KEYRING_SERVICE}')\n")
    for section in store.SECTIONS:
        print(f"[{section.title}]")
        for f in section.fields:
            val = store.get(f.key)
            if val:
                shown = store.mask(val) if f.secret else val
                print(f"  {f.key:<20} {shown:<26} ({store.location(f.key)})")
            else:
                print(f"  {f.key:<20} {'—':<26} ({'optional' if f.optional else 'MISSING'})")
        print()
    return 0


def _interactive() -> int:
    print("minima config — press Enter to keep the current value.\n")
    for section in store.SECTIONS:
        print(f"# {section.title} — {section.note}")
        for f in section.fields:
            cur = store.get(f.key)
            if f.secret:
                shown = store.mask(cur) if cur else "unset"
                entered = getpass.getpass(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            else:
                shown = cur or f.default or "unset"
                entered = input(f"  {f.key} [{shown}]: ").strip()
            if entered:
                print(f"    saved → {store.set_value(f.key, entered)}")
            elif not cur and f.default and not f.secret:
                store.set_value(f.key, f.default)
                print(f"    saved default → {f.default}")
        print()
    print("done. Run `minima config doctor` to verify.")
    return 0


def _doctor() -> int:
    store.hydrate_env()
    print("config doctor\n")
    providers = [
        ("Anthropic", "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"),
        ("Gemini", "GEMINI_API_KEY"),
        ("OpenAI", "OPENAI_API_KEY"),
        ("Mubit", "MUBIT_API_KEY"),
    ]
    for label, key in providers:
        ok = bool(os.environ.get(key))
        print(f"  [{'ok' if ok else '  '}] {label:<10} {key:<18} {'present' if ok else 'missing'}")

    url = os.environ.get("MINIMA_URL", DEFAULT_MINIMA_URL)
    print(f"\n  Minima endpoint: {url}")
    import httpx

    reachable = False
    try:
        resp = httpx.get(url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/health", timeout=5.0)
        print(f"  health: HTTP {resp.status_code}")
        reachable = True
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any failure is just 'unreachable'
        print(f"  health: unreachable ({type(exc).__name__})")

    # Authenticated probe: /v1/health is unauthenticated, so it can't tell a valid key from a
    # bad one. A minimal recommend with the key actually exercises the routing auth path.
    key = os.environ.get("MINIMA_API_KEY") or os.environ.get("MUBIT_API_KEY")
    if not reachable:
        return 0
    if not key:
        print("  auth: no MUBIT_API_KEY/MINIMA_API_KEY set — routing falls back to offline")
        return 0
    try:
        r = httpx.post(
            url.rstrip("/") + "/v1/recommend",
            headers={"authorization": f"Bearer {key}"},
            json={"task": {"task": "minima config doctor probe"}, "cost_quality_tradeoff": 5},
            timeout=15.0,

content.content:
1	# Minima
2	
3	**Recommend a cheaper LLM model for each task, so LLM-driven workflows spend fewer tokens
4	without losing the quality the task actually needs.**
5	
6	Minima **only recommends** — it never proxies a call, runs a model, rewrites a prompt, or
7	caches. It is a stack-agnostic advice layer backed by [Mubit](https://docs.mubit.ai) memory:
8	ask which model to use, run that model yourself, then tell Minima how it went. Because it
9	sits *beside* your call rather than in front of it, **it adds zero latency to your real LLM
10	request.**
11	
12	```
13	  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
14	   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce memory)
15	        ▲                                                           │
16	        └──────────────  recommendations get sharper  ─────────────┘
17	```
18	
19	## Why it works
20	
21	Minima is backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) memory. Every `POST /v1/feedback` writes a `task → model → outcome` record; every `POST /v1/recommend` recalls the most similar past records and picks the cheapest model expected to clear a quality bar. The longer it runs, the sharper the picks.
22	
23	A `cost_quality_tradeoff` slider (0 = cheapest acceptable, 10 = highest quality) moves the
24	bar. When memory is thin or conflicting, Minima can escalate to a cheap-LLM reasoner
25	(configurable, off by default).
26	
27	### Cost ranking that reflects reality
28	
29	A flat token estimate assumes a fixed completion length, so it ignores reasoning/thinking
30	tokens and mis-ranks a model with cheap list prices but heavy internal reasoning. Minima
31	ranks candidates by what they **really** cost, choosing one basis for the whole candidate
32	set:
33	
34	- **rescaled** (best) — this request's input priced + the model's *observed* output-token
35	  behavior; size-exact **and** reasoning-aware.
36	- **observed** — robust median of realized `$/call` from recalled outcomes.
37	- **estimate** (cold start) — token estimate from catalog prices.
38	
39	The basis climbs `estimate → observed → rescaled` as your `/feedback` calls accumulate
40	realized tokens and cost. See [Concepts → Cost-basis tiers](docs/concepts.md#cost-basis-tiers-estimate--observed--rescaled).
41	
42	## Endpoints
43	
44	| Endpoint | Purpose |
45	|----------|---------|
46	| `POST /v1/recommend` | Recommend a model for one task. |
47	| `POST /v1/recommend/workflow` | Recommend a model per step of a multi-step workflow. |
48	| `POST /v1/feedback` | Report an outcome and close the learning loop. |
49	| `GET  /v1/models` | The current model catalog (cost + capability priors). |
50	| `GET  /v1/strategies` | Rules Mubit has promoted for a namespace (explainability). |
51	| `GET  /v1/health` | Service, Mubit, catalog, and reasoner status. |
52	| `POST\|GET\|DELETE /v1/admin/tenants` | Tenant provisioning (multi-tenant mode only). |
53	
54	Full schemas, fields, warnings, and error formats: **[API Reference](docs/api-reference.md)**.
55	
56	## Quickstart
57	
58	```bash
59	uv sync --extra dev
60	cp .env.example .env                       # set MUBIT_API_KEY (+ MUBIT_ENDPOINT if not local)
61	
62	# optional: seed cold-start memory so day-one picks are grounded
63	uv run minima-seed --dataset synthetic --limit 2000 --lane minima:default
64	
65	make run                                   # uvicorn on :8080 (interactive docs at /docs)
66	```
67	
68	```bash
69	# recommend
70	curl -s localhost:8080/v1/recommend -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
71	  "task": {"task": "Summarize this incident report into 3 bullets.",
72	           "task_type": "summarization"},
73	  "cost_quality_tradeoff": 3
74	}' | jq
75	
76	# ...run the recommended model yourself, then close the loop
77	curl -s localhost:8080/v1/feedback -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
78	  "recommendation_id": "<from above>", "chosen_model_id": "claude-haiku-4-5",
79	  "outcome": "success", "quality_score": 0.95,
80	  "input_tokens": 1760, "output_tokens": 110, "actual_cost_usd": 0.0021,
81	  "verified_in_production": true
82	}' | jq
83	```
84	
85	Minima talks to a Mubit runtime at `MUBIT_ENDPOINT` (defaults to `http://127.0.0.1:3000`;
86	start one with `make run-mubit` in the Mubit repo) and uses Mubit's server-side embeddings,
87	so it needs no embedding model of its own.
88	
89	## Python client
90	
91	```python
92	from minima_client import MinimaClient
93	
94	with MinimaClient("http://localhost:8080") as minima:
95	    rec = minima.recommend("Write a Python CSV parser.", cost_quality_tradeoff=3)
96	    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id yourself ...
97	    minima.feedback(rec.recommendation_id, rec.recommended_model.model_id, "success",
98	                    quality_score=0.95, input_tokens=180, output_tokens=640,
99	                    actual_cost_usd=0.0034, verified_in_production=True)
100	```
101	
102	Sync + async clients and zero-code `autocapture`: **[Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md)**.
103	
104	## Documentation
105	
106	| Doc | What's in it |
107	|-----|--------------|
108	| [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md) | Install, configure, run, first recommendation. |
109	| [Concepts](docs/concepts.md) | The loop, the algorithm, cost-basis tiers, escalation, how it improves. |
110	| [API Reference](docs/api-reference.md) | Every endpoint, full schemas, warnings, errors. |
111	| [Configuration](docs/configuration.md) | Every environment variable + tuning guidance. |
112	| [Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md) | `minima_client` clients + autocapture. |
113	| [Cold-Start Seeding](docs/seeding.md) | Load history so day-one picks are grounded. |
114	| [Multi-Tenancy](docs/multi-tenancy.md) | One deployment, many orgs, per-org Mubit instances. |
115	| [Operations](docs/operations.md) | Deployment, health, degradation, monitoring, secrets. |
116	| [Examples](docs/examples.md) | Guided tour of the runnable examples. |
117	| [Agent Harness](docs/harness.md) | `minima_harness`: a Minima-routing port of PI's agent toolkit. |
118	
119	## Examples
120	
121	Runnable, progressively advanced — in **[`examples/`](examples/)**:
122	
123	| # | Example | Shows |
124	|---|---------|-------|
125	| 1 | [`01_quickstart.sh`](examples/01_quickstart.sh) | Raw `curl` against every endpoint. |
126	| 2 | [`02_recommend_and_feedback.py`](examples/02_recommend_and_feedback.py) | The core loop with the SDK. |
127	| 3 | [`03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py`](examples/03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py) | Constraints + slider sweep. |
128	| 4 | [`04_workflow.py`](examples/04_workflow.py) | Per-step workflow recommendations. |
129	| 5 | [`05_autocapture.py`](examples/05_autocapture.py) | Zero-code intake via `mubit.learn`. |
130	| 6 | [`06_routed_llm_call.py`](examples/06_routed_llm_call.py) | Routing a real Claude call + feedback. |
131	| 7 | [`07_multitenant_admin.py`](examples/07_multitenant_admin.py) | Provision an org, call as that tenant. |
132	| 8 | [`harness_warmup.py`](examples/harness_warmup.py) | The `minima_harness` agent loop (demo mode needs no keys). |
133	
134	## Agent harness
135	
136	[`minima_harness/`](src/minima_harness) is a lean Python port of
137	[`@earendil-works/pi`](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi)'s agent toolkit, made
138	Minima-native: an `Agent` runtime with tool calling **plus** a `MinimaAgent` that routes
139	every prompt through Minima and feeds the realized tokens/cost/quality back. It is the
140	"run the model yourself" half of the Minima loop, packaged.
141	
142	```python
143	from minima_harness.minima import MinimaAgent, HarnessConfig
144	
145	agent = MinimaAgent(HarnessConfig.from_env())   # MINIMA_URL, candidates, judge policy
146	await agent.prompt("Summarize this incident.", task_type="summarization", slider=3)
147	# -> Minima picked the model, the agent ran it, judged quality, fed the outcome back
148	```
149	
150	Try it with no keys via the in-process demo:
151	
152	```bash
153	uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py          # demo (in-process Minima + fake provider)
154	uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py --live   # real Minima + real providers
155	```
156	
157	Full architecture, the loop mapping, and extension guide:
158	**[Agent Harness](docs/harness.md)**.
159	
160	## Configuration
161	
162	All configuration is via environment variables (see [`.env.example`](.env.example) and
163	[Configuration](docs/configuration.md)). The only required value is `MUBIT_API_KEY` (in
164	single-tenant mode). Notable knobs:
165	
166	- `MINIMA_USE_OBSERVED_COST` / `MINIMA_OBSERVED_COST_MIN_N` — rank by realized cost.
167	- `MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER` — enable the cheap-LLM escalation tier (`anthropic` / `gemini`).
168	- `MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=sqlite` — durable recommendation resolution (multi-worker).
169	- `MINIMA_MULTITENANT` — serve many orgs from one deployment.
170	
171	## Development
172	
173	```bash
174	make install     # uv sync --extra dev
175	make test        # unit + integration (no Mubit needed)
176	make lint        # ruff + mypy
177	make live        # end-to-end against a running Mubit (pytest -m live)
178	make eval        # offline RouterBench savings evaluation (pytest -m eval)
179	make fmt         # ruff --fix + format
180	make seed        # minima-seed (LIMIT=, LANE= overridable)
181	```
182	
183	## Project layout
184	
185	```
186	src/minima/
187	  api/routers/      recommend · feedback · models · strategies · health · admin
188	  recommender/      engine · classify · aggregate · score · escalation · propensity · recstore
189	  memory/           adapter (only Mubit touchpoint) · records · keys · threadpool
190	  catalog/          store · merge · refresh · sources/{litellm,openrouter} · data/*.json
191	  llm/              base · anthropic · gemini · registry   (the escalation reasoner)
192	  tenancy/          runtime · registry · context · keys · secrets
193	  seeding/          routerbench · synthetic · run_seed (minima-seed CLI)
194	  schemas/          common · recommend · workflow · feedback · models_catalog · strategies · admin
195	src/minima_harness/   ported pi-ai (ai/) + pi-agent-core (agent/) + Minima integration (minima/) — see docs/harness.md
196	client_sdk/minima_client/   client (sync+async) · autocapture · errors
197	docs/               full documentation       examples/   runnable examples
198	tests/              unit · integration (FakeMemory) · live (-m live) · eval (-m eval)
199	```
200	
201	## License
202	
203	Minima is **source-available** under the [Functional Source License,
204	Version 1.1, Apache 2.0 Future License](LICENSE) (`FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0`).
205	
206	You may use, copy, modify, and self-host Minima for any **Permitted Purpose** —
207	internal use, non-commercial education/research, and professional services for a
208	licensee. The one restriction is a **Competing Use**: you may not offer Minima

210	offering that competes with us. Two years after each version is published, that

# Minima

**Recommend a cheaper LLM model for each task, so LLM-driven workflows spend fewer tokens
without losing the quality the task actually needs.**

Minima **only recommends** — it never proxies a call, runs a model, rewrites a prompt, or
caches. It is a stack-agnostic advice layer backed by [Mubit](https://docs.mubit.ai) memory:
ask which model to use, run that model yourself, then tell Minima how it went. Because it
sits *beside* your call rather than in front of it, **it adds zero latency to your real LLM
request.**

```
  POST /v1/recommend  ──▶  you run the model  ──▶  POST /v1/feedback
   (recall + rank)          (your stack)            (write outcome, reinforce memory)
        ▲                                                           │
        └──────────────  recommendations get sharper  ─────────────┘
```

## Why it works

Minima is backed by [Mubit](https://mubit.ai) memory. Every `POST /v1/feedback` writes a `task → model → outcome` record; every `POST /v1/recommend` recalls the most similar past records and picks the cheapest model expected to clear a quality bar. The longer it runs, the sharper the picks.

A `cost_quality_tradeoff` slider (0 = cheapest acceptable, 10 = highest quality) moves the
bar. When memory is thin or conflicting, Minima can escalate to a cheap-LLM reasoner
(configurable, off by default).

### Cost ranking that reflects reality

A flat token estimate assumes a fixed completion length, so it ignores reasoning/thinking
tokens and mis-ranks a model with cheap list prices but heavy internal reasoning. Minima
ranks candidates by what they **really** cost, choosing one basis for the whole candidate
set:

- **rescaled** (best) — this request's input priced + the model's *observed* output-token
  behavior; size-exact **and** reasoning-aware.
- **observed** — robust median of realized `$/call` from recalled outcomes.
- **estimate** (cold start) — token estimate from catalog prices.

The basis climbs `estimate → observed → rescaled` as your `/feedback` calls accumulate
realized tokens and cost. See [Concepts → Cost-basis tiers](docs/concepts.md#cost-basis-tiers-estimate--observed--rescaled).

## Endpoints

| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `POST /v1/recommend` | Recommend a model for one task. |
| `POST /v1/recommend/workflow` | Recommend a model per step of a multi-step workflow. |
| `POST /v1/feedback` | Report an outcome and close the learning loop. |
| `GET  /v1/models` | The current model catalog (cost + capability priors). |
| `GET  /v1/strategies` | Rules Mubit has promoted for a namespace (explainability). |
| `GET  /v1/health` | Service, Mubit, catalog, and reasoner status. |
| `POST\|GET\|DELETE /v1/admin/tenants` | Tenant provisioning (multi-tenant mode only). |

Full schemas, fields, warnings, and error formats: **[API Reference](docs/api-reference.md)**.

## Quickstart

```bash
uv sync --extra dev
cp .env.example .env                       # set MUBIT_API_KEY (+ MUBIT_ENDPOINT if not local)

# optional: seed cold-start memory so day-one picks are grounded
uv run minima-seed --dataset synthetic --limit 2000 --lane minima:default

make run                                   # uvicorn on :8080 (interactive docs at /docs)
```

```bash
# recommend
curl -s localhost:8080/v1/recommend -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
  "task": {"task": "Summarize this incident report into 3 bullets.",
           "task_type": "summarization"},
  "cost_quality_tradeoff": 3
}' | jq

# ...run the recommended model yourself, then close the loop
curl -s localhost:8080/v1/feedback -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{
  "recommendation_id": "<from above>", "chosen_model_id": "claude-haiku-4-5",
  "outcome": "success", "quality_score": 0.95,
  "input_tokens": 1760, "output_tokens": 110, "actual_cost_usd": 0.0021,
  "verified_in_production": true
}' | jq
```

Minima talks to a Mubit runtime at `MUBIT_ENDPOINT` (defaults to `http://127.0.0.1:3000`;
start one with `make run-mubit` in the Mubit repo) and uses Mubit's server-side embeddings,
so it needs no embedding model of its own.

## Python client

```python
from minima_client import MinimaClient

with MinimaClient("http://localhost:8080") as minima:
    rec = minima.recommend("Write a Python CSV parser.", cost_quality_tradeoff=3)
    # ... run rec.recommended_model.model_id yourself ...
    minima.feedback(rec.recommendation_id, rec.recommended_model.model_id, "success",
                    quality_score=0.95, input_tokens=180, output_tokens=640,
                    actual_cost_usd=0.0034, verified_in_production=True)
```

Sync + async clients and zero-code `autocapture`: **[Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md)**.

## Documentation

| Doc | What's in it |
|-----|--------------|
| [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md) | Install, configure, run, first recommendation. |
| [Concepts](docs/concepts.md) | The loop, the algorithm, cost-basis tiers, escalation, how it improves. |
| [API Reference](docs/api-reference.md) | Every endpoint, full schemas, warnings, errors. |
| [Configuration](docs/configuration.md) | Every environment variable + tuning guidance. |
| [Python Client SDK](docs/client-sdk.md) | `minima_client` clients + autocapture. |
| [Cold-Start Seeding](docs/seeding.md) | Load history so day-one picks are grounded. |
| [Multi-Tenancy](docs/multi-tenancy.md) | One deployment, many orgs, per-org Mubit instances. |
| [Operations](docs/operations.md) | Deployment, health, degradation, monitoring, secrets. |
| [Examples](docs/examples.md) | Guided tour of the runnable examples. |
| [Agent Harness](docs/harness.md) | `minima_harness`: a Minima-routing port of PI's agent toolkit. |

## Examples

Runnable, progressively advanced — in **[`examples/`](examples/)**:

| # | Example | Shows |
|---|---------|-------|
| 1 | [`01_quickstart.sh`](examples/01_quickstart.sh) | Raw `curl` against every endpoint. |
| 2 | [`02_recommend_and_feedback.py`](examples/02_recommend_and_feedback.py) | The core loop with the SDK. |
| 3 | [`03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py`](examples/03_constraints_and_tradeoff.py) | Constraints + slider sweep. |
| 4 | [`04_workflow.py`](examples/04_workflow.py) | Per-step workflow recommendations. |
| 5 | [`05_autocapture.py`](examples/05_autocapture.py) | Zero-code intake via `mubit.learn`. |
| 6 | [`06_routed_llm_call.py`](examples/06_routed_llm_call.py) | Routing a real Claude call + feedback. |
| 7 | [`07_multitenant_admin.py`](examples/07_multitenant_admin.py) | Provision an org, call as that tenant. |
| 8 | [`harness_warmup.py`](examples/harness_warmup.py) | The `minima_harness` agent loop (demo mode needs no keys). |

## Agent harness

[`minima_harness/`](src/minima_harness) is a lean Python port of
[`@earendil-works/pi`](https://github.com/earendil-works/pi)'s agent toolkit, made
Minima-native: an `Agent` runtime with tool calling **plus** a `MinimaAgent` that routes
every prompt through Minima and feeds the realized tokens/cost/quality back. It is the
"run the model yourself" half of the Minima loop, packaged.

```python
from minima_harness.minima import MinimaAgent, HarnessConfig

agent = MinimaAgent(HarnessConfig.from_env())   # MINIMA_URL, candidates, judge policy
await agent.prompt("Summarize this incident.", task_type="summarization", slider=3)
# -> Minima picked the model, the agent ran it, judged quality, fed the outcome back
```

Try it with no keys via the in-process demo:

```bash
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py          # demo (in-process Minima + fake provider)
uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py --live   # real Minima + real providers
```

Full architecture, the loop mapping, and extension guide:
**[Agent Harness](docs/harness.md)**.

## Configuration

All configuration is via environment variables (see [`.env.example`](.env.example) and
[Configuration](docs/configuration.md)). The only required value is `MUBIT_API_KEY` (in
single-tenant mode). Notable knobs:

- `MINIMA_USE_OBSERVED_COST` / `MINIMA_OBSERVED_COST_MIN_N` — rank by realized cost.
- `MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER` — enable the cheap-LLM escalation tier (`anthropic` / `gemini`).
- `MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=sqlite` — durable recommendation resolution (multi-worker).
- `MINIMA_MULTITENANT` — serve many orgs from one deployment.

## Development

```bash
make install     # uv sync --extra dev
make test        # unit + integration (no Mubit needed)
make lint        # ruff + mypy
make live        # end-to-end against a running Mubit (pytest -m live)
make eval        # offline RouterBench savings evaluation (pytest -m eval)
make fmt         # ruff --fix + format
make seed        # minima-seed (LIMIT=, LANE= overridable)
```

## Project layout

```
src/minima/
  api/routers/      recommend · feedback · models · strategies · health · admin
  recommender/      engine · classify · aggregate · score · escalation · propensity · recstore
  memory/           adapter (only Mubit touchpoint) · records · keys · threadpool
  catalog/          store · merge · refresh · sources/{litellm,openrouter} · data/*.json
  llm/              base · anthropic · gemini · registry   (the escalation reasoner)
  tenancy/          runtime · registry · context · keys · secrets
  seeding/          routerbench · synthetic · run_seed (minima-seed CLI)
  schemas/          common · recommend · workflow · feedback · models_catalog · strategies · admin
src/minima_harness/   ported pi-ai (ai/) + pi-agent-core (agent/) + Minima integration (minima/) — see docs/harness.md
client_sdk/minima_client/   client (sync+async) · autocapture · errors
docs/               full documentation       examples/   runnable examples
tests/              unit · integration (FakeMemory) · live (-m live) · eval (-m eval)
```

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any

from minima_harness.agent.events import (
    AgentEndEvent,
    AgentStartEvent,
    MessageEndEvent,
    MessageStartEvent,
    MessageUpdateEvent,
    ToolExecutionEndEvent,
    ToolExecutionStartEvent,
    ToolExecutionUpdateEvent,
    TurnEndEvent,
    TurnStartEvent,
)
from minima_harness.ai.events import TextDeltaEvent, ThinkingDeltaEvent

_log = logging.getLogger("minima_harness.tui.bridge")


@dataclass
class EventBridge:
    """Agent subscribe-listener that turns AgentEvents into a renderable transcript model.

    The app passes ``on_*`` callbacks (wired to widgets) via :meth:`bind`; absent
    callbacks are no-ops so the bridge is unit-testable in isolation.
    """

    assistant_text: str = field(default="")
    tools: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    turns: int = 0
    finished: bool = False
    error: str | None = None

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        self._on_text = None
        self._on_thinking = None
        self._on_tool_start = None
        self._on_tool_end = None
        self._on_turn = None
        self._on_finish = None

    def bind(
        self,
        *,
        on_text=None,
        on_thinking=None,
        on_tool_start=None,
        on_tool_end=None,
        on_turn=None,
        on_finish=None,
    ) -> None:
        self._on_text = on_text
        self._on_thinking = on_thinking
        self._on_tool_start = on_tool_start
        self._on_tool_end = on_tool_end
        self._on_turn = on_turn
        self._on_finish = on_finish

    def _safe(self, cb, *args) -> None:
        if cb is None:
            return
        try:
            cb(*args)
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - the hot path must never break on rendering
            _log.warning("bridge_callback_failed", exc_info=True)

    async def __call__(self, event) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001 - AgentEvent union
        if isinstance(event, AgentStartEvent):
            self.assistant_text = ""
            self.tools = []
            self.turns = 0
            self.finished = False
        elif isinstance(event, TurnStartEvent):
            pass
        elif isinstance(event, MessageStartEvent):
            pass
        elif isinstance(event, MessageUpdateEvent):
            stream = event.assistant_message_event
            if isinstance(stream, TextDeltaEvent) and stream.delta:
                self.assistant_text += stream.delta
                self._safe(self._on_text, stream.delta)
            elif isinstance(stream, ThinkingDeltaEvent) and stream.delta:
                self._safe(self._on_thinking, stream.delta)
        elif isinstance(event, MessageEndEvent):
            pass
        elif isinstance(event, ToolExecutionStartEvent):
            rec = {"id": event.tool_call_id, "name": event.tool_name, "args": event.args}
            self.tools.append(rec)
            self._safe(self._on_tool_start, rec)
        elif isinstance(event, ToolExecutionUpdateEvent):
            pass
        elif isinstance(event, ToolExecutionEndEvent):
            self._safe(self._on_tool_end, event.tool_call_id, event.result, event.is_error)
        elif isinstance(event, TurnEndEvent):
            self.turns += 1
            self._safe(self._on_turn, event)

9	from minima_harness.minima import HarnessConfig, MinimaAgent

25	        agent = MinimaAgent(_offline_cfg(), model=faux)

file.content:
"""run_print/run_json surface provider errors instead of printing a silent blank line."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio

from minima_harness.ai import AssistantMessage, TextContent
from minima_harness.ai.providers import register_faux_provider
from minima_harness.minima import HarnessConfig, MinimaAgent
from minima_harness.tui.run_modes import run_print


def _msg(text: str) -> AssistantMessage:
    return AssistantMessage(content=[TextContent(text=text)])


def _offline_cfg() -> HarnessConfig:
    # minima_url="" -> routing disabled -> runs the fixed model with no router needed.
    return HarnessConfig(minima_url="", candidates=["faux"], judge_every=0, allow_offline=True)


def test_run_print_reports_provider_error(capsys):
    with register_faux_provider() as reg:
        faux = reg.get_model()  # no responses queued -> provider error turn
        agent = MinimaAgent(_offline_cfg(), model=faux)
        rc = asyncio.run(run_print(agent, "hi"))
    captured = capsys.readouterr()
    assert rc == 1  # non-zero exit on failure
    assert captured.err.strip()  # the reason went to stderr
    assert captured.out.strip() == ""  # nothing misleading on stdout


def test_run_print_prints_answer_on_success(capsys):
    with register_faux_provider() as reg:
        reg.set_responses([_msg("the answer")])
        faux = reg.get_model()
        agent = MinimaAgent(_offline_cfg(), model=faux)
        rc = asyncio.run(run_print(agent, "hi"))
    captured = capsys.readouterr()
    assert rc == 0
    assert "the answer" in captured.out

1	# --- Mubit memory backend ---
2	# Auth is pass-through: callers present their own Mubit key as Authorization: Bearer.
3	# MUBIT_API_KEY here is only the local/dev default (single-key mode); hosted Minima
4	# never stores caller keys.
5	# Leave MUBIT_ENDPOINT unset for the hosted default (https://api.mubit.ai). Uncomment and
6	# point it at a LOCAL Mubit runtime ONLY for server development — a stray localhost value
7	# copied into ./.env would silently degrade the `minima` CLI's memory to offline.
8	# MUBIT_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:3000
9	MUBIT_API_KEY=
10	# http is the proven transport for the local runtime: recall uses mode=direct_bypass,
11	# which the runtime's gRPC QueryMode enum does not accept (auto may pick gRPC and fail).
12	# NOTE: recall (mode=direct_bypass) requires the org's instance to have
13	# enable_direct_search=true (default false on hosted Mubit instances).
14	MUBIT_TRANSPORT=http
15	MUBIT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000
16	
17	# --- Memory read path ---
18	# Recall includes a server-side embedding round-trip; latency is embedder-bound
19	# (~100-300ms on a GPU embedder, ~1.5s on a local CPU one). Bounds recommendation latency.
20	MINIMA_MEMORY_RECALL_TIMEOUT_MS=2500
21	MINIMA_MEMORY_RECALL_LIMIT=25
22	# agent_routed works on all Mubit instances including hosted api.mubit.ai.
23	# direct_bypass is lower-latency but requires enable_direct_search=true on the instance
24	# (default false on hosted Mubit; safe to use with a local Mubit runtime).
25	MINIMA_RECALL_MODE=agent_routed
26	MINIMA_LANE_PREFIX=minima
27	MINIMA_SEED_LANE=minima:default
28	# Server-side recall precision (all have legacy escape hatches: empty string = off).
29	# Entry types covering both intake paths (seeds=fact, feedback=observation).
30	MINIMA_RECALL_ENTRY_TYPES=fact,observation
31	# relevance | freshness | balanced — balanced lets recency influence WHICH neighbors
32	# arrive; per-neighbor age weighting is the client-side half-life decay below.
33	MINIMA_RECALL_RANK_BY=balanced
34	# Hard recency window in days (0 = no window).
35	MINIMA_RECALL_MAX_AGE_DAYS=0
36	# Mubit search budget tier: low | mid | high.
37	MINIMA_RECALL_BUDGET=mid
38	# Log per-evidence score breakdowns (diagnostic; keep off in prod).
39	MINIMA_RECALL_EXPLAIN=false
40	
41	# --- Recommender tuning ---
42	MINIMA_TAU_MIN=0.55
43	MINIMA_TAU_MAX=0.92
44	MINIMA_BETA_PSEUDOCOUNT=2.5
45	MINIMA_ESCALATION_W_MIN=1.5
46	MINIMA_ESCALATION_N_MIN=3
47	MINIMA_ESCALATION_C_MIN=0.45
48	MINIMA_ESCALATION_TIE_DELTA=0.05
49	# legacy = four independent triggers; uncertainty = posterior-interval gate replaces
50	# thin_evidence+low_confidence (conflict stays a hard override). Shadow before switching.
51	MINIMA_ESCALATION_MODE=legacy
52	MINIMA_ESCALATION_INTERVAL_WIDTH=0.25
53	MINIMA_DEFAULT_INPUT_TOKENS=[REDACTED]
54	MINIMA_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_TOKENS=500
55	MINIMA_REFLECT_EVERY_N=25
56	# Rank candidates by what they REALLY cost, not a flat token estimate that ignores
57	# reasoning/thinking tokens. With >=MIN_N observations per candidate the engine re-scales the
58	# model's observed output behaviour to this request's input size (size-exact + reasoning-aware);
59	# with cost-but-no-token history it uses the robust median realized $/call; otherwise it falls
60	# back to the token estimate. One basis is chosen for the whole candidate set (no apples-to-oranges).
61	MINIMA_USE_OBSERVED_COST=true
62	MINIMA_OBSERVED_COST_MIN_N=3
63	# Evidence age decay: a recalled outcome's weight halves every half-life (records
64	# without a timestamp keep the legacy binary stale penalty). Floor bounds old evidence.
65	MINIMA_EVIDENCE_HALF_LIFE_DAYS=30.0
66	MINIMA_EVIDENCE_DECAY_FLOOR=0.1
67	# Seeded outcomes count at SEED_WEIGHT, crowded out linearly once a model has
68	# CROWDOUT_N live outcomes in the recalled set. 1.0/0 = legacy (seeds at full weight).
69	MINIMA_SEED_WEIGHT=0.5
70	MINIMA_SEED_CROWDOUT_N=5
71	# Latency annotation/enforcement (Constraints.max_latency_ms): robust observed
72	# percentile, only for candidates with >= MIN_N latency observations.
73	MINIMA_LATENCY_PERCENTILE=0.75
74	MINIMA_LATENCY_MIN_N=3
75	# Default-output-token multipliers by classified difficulty (JSON dict), applied to
76	# MINIMA_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_TOKENS when the caller omits expected_output_tokens.
77	# Affects the "estimate" cost basis only.
78	MINIMA_DIFFICULTY_OUTPUT_MULTIPLIERS={"trivial": 0.5, "easy": 0.75, "medium": 1.0, "hard": 1.5, "expert": 2.0}
79	
80	# --- Cheap-LLM reasoner (recommend-only); set provider=anthropic|gemini + the matching key ---
81	# Requires the provider extra: `uv sync --extra reasoner-anthropic` and/or `--extra reasoner-gemini`.
82	MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER=none
83	MINIMA_REASONER_MODEL=
84	MINIMA_REASONER_TIMEOUT_MS=15000
85	# Output cap. Gemini 3.x reasons before emitting JSON; <4096 can truncate the response.
86	MINIMA_REASONER_MAX_TOKENS=[REDACTED]
87	MINIMA_REASONER_BLEND=0.5
88	# Adaptive blend: weight the LLM's estimate by how thin the deterministic evidence is
89	# (cold candidates lean on the LLM; well-evidenced ones barely move). false = fixed blend.
90	MINIMA_REASONER_BLEND_ADAPTIVE=true
91	MINIMA_REASONER_BLEND_MAX=0.8
92	MINIMA_REASONER_CLASSIFY=true
93	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=
94	GEMINI_API_KEY=
95	
96	# --- Selection-bias correction (inverse propensity weighting) ---
97	MINIMA_IPW_ENABLED=true
98	MINIMA_IPW_CLIP_LOW=0.1
99	MINIMA_IPW_CLIP_HIGH=10.0
100	
101	# --- Learning maturity (Phase 3) ---
102	# Cluster granularity for the per-(cluster, model) upsert key. coarse = task_type:difficulty;
103	# fine appends a salient-keyword signature so distinct topics accumulate separately.
104	MINIMA_CLUSTER_GRANULARITY=coarse
105	MINIMA_CLUSTER_SIGNATURE_TOKENS=4
106	# Promote a verified-in-production strong success to a durable Lesson (feeds reflect()).
107	MINIMA_LESSON_ON_VERIFIED_PROD=true
108	MINIMA_LESSON_MIN_QUALITY=0.8
109	# Optimistic bonus for under-explored candidates (0.0 = off; pure exploitation).
110	MINIMA_EXPLORATION_BONUS=0.0
111	
112	# --- Catalog ---
113	MINIMA_CATALOG_REFRESH_SECONDS=21600
114	MINIMA_CATALOG_STALE_AFTER_SECONDS=86400
115	MINIMA_LITELLM_PRICES_URL=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/model_prices_and_context_window.json
116	MINIMA_OPENROUTER_MODELS_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models
117	OPENROUTER_API_KEY=
118	
119	# --- Service ---
120	MINIMA_HOST=0.0.0.0
121	# NOTE: a local Mubit embedding server commonly runs on :8080; pick another port for Minima.
122	MINIMA_PORT=8088
123	MINIMA_LOG_LEVEL=info
124	# memory | sqlite | cloudsql — controls DecisionLog, Propensity, and RecStore + DurableRefs
125	# unless MINIMA_RECSTORE_BACKEND overrides the operational stores.
126	#   memory   — in-process; lost on restart. Fine for local dev.
127	#   sqlite   — SQLite on MINIMA_SQLITE_PATH. NOTE: /tmp is ephemeral on Cloud Run.
128	#   cloudsql — PostgreSQL via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy (requires MINIMA_DATABASE_URL).
129	MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=memory
130	# 7 days: feedback often arrives late (batch evals, prod verification). After expiry the
131	# late-feedback path still accepts outcomes (without neighbor attribution).
132	MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_TTL_SECONDS=604800
133	MINIMA_SQLITE_PATH=minima_state.db
134	MINIMA_LATE_FEEDBACK_ENABLED=true
135	
136	# --- Persistent store backends (Cloud SQL + Redis) ---
137	# PostgreSQL DSN — required when MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=cloudsql.
138	# Cloud Run (Auth Proxy socket): postgresql://app_user:PASSWORD@/minima?host=/cloudsql/PROJECT:REGION:INSTANCE
139	# Local (TCP):                   postgresql://app_user:PASSWORD@localhost:5432/minima
140	MINIMA_DATABASE_URL=
141	# Redis URL — required when MINIMA_RECSTORE_BACKEND=redis (Cloud Memorystore private IP).
142	MINIMA_REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0
143	# Override backend for RecStore + DurableRefs independently of MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE.
144	#   redis    — Redis/Memorystore (TTL-native, low-latency); requires MINIMA_REDIS_URL
145	#   cloudsql — PostgreSQL (same DB as DecisionLog/Propensity)
146	#   empty    — inherit from MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE (default)
147	MINIMA_RECSTORE_BACKEND=
148	
149	# --- Decision logging & off-policy evaluation ---
150	# Every recommendation is logged (candidate set, propensity vector, tau, cost baselines)
151	# and reconciled at feedback. Powers GET /v1/savings, GET /v1/calibration, feedback
152	# coverage, and the minima-calibration-report script.
153	MINIMA_DECISION_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS=90
154	# Comma-separated org ids opted into epsilon-stochastic selection (sampling within the
155	# tau-eligible set). Empty = deterministic argmin everywhere (default).
156	MINIMA_EPSILON_SELECTION_ORGS=
157	MINIMA_EPSILON=0.03
158	MINIMA_EPSILON_SOFTMAX_TEMPERATURE=0.1
159	
160	# --- Calibration monitoring ---
161	MINIMA_CALIBRATION_WINDOW_DAYS=30
162	MINIMA_CALIBRATION_SHRINKAGE_K=20.0
163	MINIMA_CALIBRATION_BINS=10
164	# CUSUM drift detection, sized for binary residuals (smaller values flag healthy streams).
165	MINIMA_CUSUM_K=0.25
166	MINIMA_CUSUM_H=2.0
167	
168	# --- Durable-record fast path ---
169	# off | shadow (fetch + log what ANN missed, don't merge) | on (merge into evidence).
170	MINIMA_DURABLE_FASTPATH=off
171	MINIMA_DURABLE_FASTPATH_MAX_REFS=8
172	
173	# --- Tenancy ---
174	# Auth is pass-through (the caller's Mubit key IS the credential; org id is derived from
175	# it). This org id only partitions single-key/dev state (recstore/propensity/decisions).
176	MINIMA_DEFAULT_ORG_ID=default
177

file.content:
# --- Mubit memory backend ---
# Auth is pass-through: callers present their own Mubit key as Authorization: Bearer.
# MUBIT_API_KEY here is only the local/dev default (single-key mode); hosted Minima
# never stores caller keys.
# Leave MUBIT_ENDPOINT unset for the hosted default (https://api.mubit.ai). Uncomment and
# point it at a LOCAL Mubit runtime ONLY for server development — a stray localhost value
# copied into ./.env would silently degrade the `minima` CLI's memory to offline.
# MUBIT_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:3000
MUBIT_API_KEY=
# http is the proven transport for the local runtime: recall uses mode=direct_bypass,
# which the runtime's gRPC QueryMode enum does not accept (auto may pick gRPC and fail).
# NOTE: recall (mode=direct_bypass) requires the org's instance to have
# enable_direct_search=true (default false on hosted Mubit instances).
MUBIT_TRANSPORT=http
MUBIT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000

# --- Memory read path ---
# Recall includes a server-side embedding round-trip; latency is embedder-bound
# (~100-300ms on a GPU embedder, ~1.5s on a local CPU one). Bounds recommendation latency.
MINIMA_MEMORY_RECALL_TIMEOUT_MS=2500
MINIMA_MEMORY_RECALL_LIMIT=25
# agent_routed works on all Mubit instances including hosted api.mubit.ai.
# direct_bypass is lower-latency but requires enable_direct_search=true on the instance
# (default false on hosted Mubit; safe to use with a local Mubit runtime).
MINIMA_RECALL_MODE=agent_routed
MINIMA_LANE_PREFIX=minima
MINIMA_SEED_LANE=minima:default
# Server-side recall precision (all have legacy escape hatches: empty string = off).
# Entry types covering both intake paths (seeds=fact, feedback=observation).
MINIMA_RECALL_ENTRY_TYPES=fact,observation
# relevance | freshness | balanced — balanced lets recency influence WHICH neighbors
# arrive; per-neighbor age weighting is the client-side half-life decay below.
MINIMA_RECALL_RANK_BY=balanced
# Hard recency window in days (0 = no window).
MINIMA_RECALL_MAX_AGE_DAYS=0
# Mubit search budget tier: low | mid | high.
MINIMA_RECALL_BUDGET=mid
# Log per-evidence score breakdowns (diagnostic; keep off in prod).
MINIMA_RECALL_EXPLAIN=false

# --- Recommender tuning ---
MINIMA_TAU_MIN=0.55
MINIMA_TAU_MAX=0.92
MINIMA_BETA_PSEUDOCOUNT=2.5
MINIMA_ESCALATION_W_MIN=1.5
MINIMA_ESCALATION_N_MIN=3
MINIMA_ESCALATION_C_MIN=0.45
MINIMA_ESCALATION_TIE_DELTA=0.05
# legacy = four independent triggers; uncertainty = posterior-interval gate replaces
# thin_evidence+low_confidence (conflict stays a hard override). Shadow before switching.
MINIMA_ESCALATION_MODE=legacy
MINIMA_ESCALATION_INTERVAL_WIDTH=0.25
MINIMA_DEFAULT_INPUT_TOKENS=[REDACTED]
MINIMA_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_TOKENS=500
MINIMA_REFLECT_EVERY_N=25
# Rank candidates by what they REALLY cost, not a flat token estimate that ignores
# reasoning/thinking tokens. With >=MIN_N observations per candidate the engine re-scales the
# model's observed output behaviour to this request's input size (size-exact + reasoning-aware);
# with cost-but-no-token history it uses the robust median realized $/call; otherwise it falls
# back to the token estimate. One basis is chosen for the whole candidate set (no apples-to-oranges).
MINIMA_USE_OBSERVED_COST=true
MINIMA_OBSERVED_COST_MIN_N=3
# Evidence age decay: a recalled outcome's weight halves every half-life (records
# without a timestamp keep the legacy binary stale penalty). Floor bounds old evidence.
MINIMA_EVIDENCE_HALF_LIFE_DAYS=30.0
MINIMA_EVIDENCE_DECAY_FLOOR=0.1
# Seeded outcomes count at SEED_WEIGHT, crowded out linearly once a model has
# CROWDOUT_N live outcomes in the recalled set. 1.0/0 = legacy (seeds at full weight).
MINIMA_SEED_WEIGHT=0.5
MINIMA_SEED_CROWDOUT_N=5
# Latency annotation/enforcement (Constraints.max_latency_ms): robust observed
# percentile, only for candidates with >= MIN_N latency observations.
MINIMA_LATENCY_PERCENTILE=0.75
MINIMA_LATENCY_MIN_N=3
# Default-output-token multipliers by classified difficulty (JSON dict), applied to
# MINIMA_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_TOKENS when the caller omits expected_output_tokens.
# Affects the "estimate" cost basis only.
MINIMA_DIFFICULTY_OUTPUT_MULTIPLIERS={"trivial": 0.5, "easy": 0.75, "medium": 1.0, "hard": 1.5, "expert": 2.0}

# --- Cheap-LLM reasoner (recommend-only); set provider=anthropic|gemini + the matching key ---
# Requires the provider extra: `uv sync --extra reasoner-anthropic` and/or `--extra reasoner-gemini`.
MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER=none
MINIMA_REASONER_MODEL=
MINIMA_REASONER_TIMEOUT_MS=15000
# Output cap. Gemini 3.x reasons before emitting JSON; <4096 can truncate the response.
MINIMA_REASONER_MAX_TOKENS=[REDACTED]
MINIMA_REASONER_BLEND=0.5
# Adaptive blend: weight the LLM's estimate by how thin the deterministic evidence is
# (cold candidates lean on the LLM; well-evidenced ones barely move). false = fixed blend.
MINIMA_REASONER_BLEND_ADAPTIVE=true
MINIMA_REASONER_BLEND_MAX=0.8
MINIMA_REASONER_CLASSIFY=true
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=[REDACTED]

# --- Selection-bias correction (inverse propensity weighting) ---
MINIMA_IPW_ENABLED=true
MINIMA_IPW_CLIP_LOW=0.1
MINIMA_IPW_CLIP_HIGH=10.0

# --- Learning maturity (Phase 3) ---
# Cluster granularity for the per-(cluster, model) upsert key. coarse = task_type:difficulty;
# fine appends a salient-keyword signature so distinct topics accumulate separately.
MINIMA_CLUSTER_GRANULARITY=coarse
MINIMA_CLUSTER_SIGNATURE_TOKENS=4
# Promote a verified-in-production strong success to a durable Lesson (feeds reflect()).
MINIMA_LESSON_ON_VERIFIED_PROD=true
MINIMA_LESSON_MIN_QUALITY=0.8
# Optimistic bonus for under-explored candidates (0.0 = off; pure exploitation).
MINIMA_EXPLORATION_BONUS=0.0

# --- Catalog ---
MINIMA_CATALOG_REFRESH_SECONDS=21600
MINIMA_CATALOG_STALE_AFTER_SECONDS=86400
MINIMA_LITELLM_PRICES_URL=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/model_prices_and_context_window.json
MINIMA_OPENROUTER_MODELS_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=

# --- Service ---
MINIMA_HOST=0.0.0.0
# NOTE: a local Mubit embedding server commonly runs on :8080; pick another port for Minima.
MINIMA_PORT=8088
MINIMA_LOG_LEVEL=info
# memory | sqlite | cloudsql — controls DecisionLog, Propensity, and RecStore + DurableRefs
# unless MINIMA_RECSTORE_BACKEND overrides the operational stores.
#   memory   — in-process; lost on restart. Fine for local dev.
#   sqlite   — SQLite on MINIMA_SQLITE_PATH. NOTE: /tmp is ephemeral on Cloud Run.
#   cloudsql — PostgreSQL via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy (requires MINIMA_DATABASE_URL).
MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=memory
# 7 days: feedback often arrives late (batch evals, prod verification). After expiry the
# late-feedback path still accepts outcomes (without neighbor attribution).
MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_TTL_SECONDS=604800
MINIMA_SQLITE_PATH=minima_state.db
MINIMA_LATE_FEEDBACK_ENABLED=true

# --- Persistent store backends (Cloud SQL + Redis) ---
# PostgreSQL DSN — required when MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE=cloudsql.
# Cloud Run (Auth Proxy socket): postgresql://app_user:PASSWORD@/minima?host=/cloudsql/PROJECT:REGION:INSTANCE
# Local (TCP):                   postgresql://app_user:PASSWORD@localhost:5432/minima
MINIMA_DATABASE_URL=
# Redis URL — required when MINIMA_RECSTORE_BACKEND=redis (Cloud Memorystore private IP).
MINIMA_REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0
# Override backend for RecStore + DurableRefs independently of MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE.
#   redis    — Redis/Memorystore (TTL-native, low-latency); requires MINIMA_REDIS_URL
#   cloudsql — PostgreSQL (same DB as DecisionLog/Propensity)
#   empty    — inherit from MINIMA_RECOMMENDATION_STORE (default)
MINIMA_RECSTORE_BACKEND=

# --- Decision logging & off-policy evaluation ---
# Every recommendation is logged (candidate set, propensity vector, tau, cost baselines)
# and reconciled at feedback. Powers GET /v1/savings, GET /v1/calibration, feedback
# coverage, and the minima-calibration-report script.
MINIMA_DECISION_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS=90
# Comma-separated org ids opted into epsilon-stochastic selection (sampling within the
# tau-eligible set). Empty = deterministic argmin everywhere (default).
MINIMA_EPSILON_SELECTION_ORGS=
MINIMA_EPSILON=0.03
MINIMA_EPSILON_SOFTMAX_TEMPERATURE=0.1

# --- Calibration monitoring ---
MINIMA_CALIBRATION_WINDOW_DAYS=30
MINIMA_CALIBRATION_SHRINKAGE_K=20.0
MINIMA_CALIBRATION_BINS=10
# CUSUM drift detection, sized for binary residuals (smaller values flag healthy streams).
MINIMA_CUSUM_K=0.25
MINIMA_CUSUM_H=2.0

# --- Durable-record fast path ---
# off | shadow (fetch + log what ANN missed, don't merge) | on (merge into evidence).
MINIMA_DURABLE_FASTPATH=off
MINIMA_DURABLE_FASTPATH_MAX_REFS=8

# --- Tenancy ---
# Auth is pass-through (the caller's Mubit key IS the credential; org id is derived from
# it). This org id only partitions single-key/dev state (recstore/propensity/decisions).
MINIMA_DEFAULT_ORG_ID=default

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

## Report: Minima Slash Commands

Based on my thorough search of the minima repository, here is a comprehensive report on all slash commands available in the interactive CLI/REPL.

### 1. Command Definition & Registration Locations

**Primary Files:**
- **Command Definition:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/commands.py` (lines 10-50)
  - Defines the `Command` dataclass and `CommandRegistry` class
  - Data structure: `Command(name: str, handler: Awaitable[str|None], description: str, hidden: bool)`

- **Command Registration:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/app.py` (lines 1012-1603)
  - `_build_commands()` method builds the registry
  - All 39 commands registered in a loop at lines 1560-1602
  - Dynamic dispatch in `_dispatch_command()` (lines 1605-1646)

- **Command Dispatch Handler:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/app.py` (lines 1605-1646)
  - Routes to registered commands or special patterns (skill templates, prompt templates)

- **Extension System:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/extensions.py` (lines 17-42)
  - Extensions can add custom commands via `@api.command(name, description="...")` decorator
  - Custom commands loaded from `~/.minima-harness/extensions/`, `.pi/extensions/`, and package roots

---

### 2. Complete List of Slash Commands

There are **39 explicit registered commands** plus 3 special command patterns (skills, templates, and skill setting). Here's the full list:

**Core Navigation & Session Management:**
1. `/quit` — Exit the agent
2. `/exit` — Quit Minima
3. `/clear` — Clear the transcript
4. `/banner` — Show / hide the welcome splash
5. `/new` — Start a fresh session
6. `/name` — Set the session display name
7. `/session` — Show session info
8. `/tree` — View the session tree
9. `/fork` — Fork from an entry id
10. `/clone` — Clone the current branch
11. `/resume` — Resume a session (optionally by id)

**Model & Routing Control:**
12. `/model` — Pick / pin the model (or `auto`, `unpin`, `clear` to release pin)
13. `/reconnect` — Retry Minima after an offline fallback
14. `/confirm` — Toggle routing confirm gate
15. `/escalate` — Toggle quality escalation (with optional threshold)

**Cost & Performance:**
16. `/cost` — Show the cost meter
17. `/cache` — Toggle semantic response cache
18. `/stats` — Show session analytics (last 10)
19. `/recall` — Mubit cross-session recall

**System Prompt & Configuration:**
20. `/prompt` — Inspect/edit the system prompt (Mubit + local)
21. `/optimize` — Optimize the system prompt via Mubit (save tokens)
22. `/config` — Manage API keys (LLM providers + Mubit)
23. `/skills` — List loaded skills (local + Mubit)

**Conversation & Output:**
24. `/copy` — Copy last reply (or `/copy <text>`) to clipboard
25. `/export` — Export the conversation to a Markdown file
26. `/compact` — Summarize older context

**Quality & Control Modes:**
27. `/judge` — Toggle LLM judging on/off
28. `/edits` — Force a diff review for every edit/write
29. `/yolo` — Toggle permission prompts (YOLO = off, runs without asking)
30. `/thoughts` — Toggle streaming the model's thinking
31. `/ledger` — Set/track a budgeted goal + tasks (`set <title>` · `clear` · `budget`)

**UI & Display:**
32. `/theme` — Switch theme (dark|light|file)
33. `/mouse` — Toggle mouse capture (scroll-wheel vs native text selection)
34. `/help` — List commands
35. `/commands` — Open the command palette
36. `/banner` — Show / hide the welcome splash (listed again for context)

**Extensibility & Administration:**
37. `/extensions` — List loaded extensions
38. `/reload` — Reload extensions + customization

**Hidden Aliases:**
39. `/goals` — Alias of `/ledger` (hidden, but dispatchable)

**Special Command Patterns (Not in Standard Registry):**
- `/skill:<name>` — Load a skill's instructions into the system prompt
- `/skill:set <name> <description>` — Save a Mubit skill
- `/<template-name>` — Expand a prompt template into the editor

---

### 3. Commands Showcasing Minima's Distinctive Features

The following commands highlight minima's unique capabilities:

**Memory & Cross-Session Intelligence:**
- `/recall` — Mubit cross-session recall (pulls knowledge from prior sessions)
- `/stats` — Session analytics aggregated across multiple sessions
- `/session`, `/tree`, `/fork`, `/clone`, `/resume` — Advanced session branching & memory navigation

**Intelligent Routing & Cost Optimization:**
- `/model` — Pin a model or auto-route via Minima's recommendation engine
- `/confirm` — Interactive routing confirmation (shows tradeoff panel before each turn)
- `/escalate` — Quality escalation with configurable thresholds
- `/cost` — Real-time cost meter with actual vs. estimated tracking
- `/cache` — Semantic response caching to reduce redundant API calls

**Prompt Intelligence & Optimization:**
- `/prompt` — Inspect layered system prompt composition (Mubit + local + skills)
- `/optimize` — AI-driven prompt optimization for token savings via Mubit
- `/skills` — Load reusable skill instructions (from local files or Mubit library)

**Judging & Quality Control:**
- `/judge` — Toggle LLM quality judging for outcome evaluation
- `/edits` — Require confirmation for agent edit/write operations
- `/thoughts` — Stream model reasoning (extended thinking)

**Budgeted Goal Tracking:**
- `/ledger` — Set/track budgeted goals with cost awareness and task checklists

---

### 4. Data Structure and Metadata Storage

**Command Data Structure:**
All commands are stored in a `CommandRegistry` (defined in `commands.py`):

```python
@dataclass(slots=True)
class Command:
    name: str
    handler: Handler  # async def(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None
    description: str = ""
    hidden: bool = False  # For aliases like /goals
```

**Registry Storage:**
```python
@dataclass(slots=True)
class CommandRegistry:
    _cmds: dict[str, Command] = field(default_factory=dict)
    
    def register(self, name: str, *, description: str = "", hidden: bool = False) -> Callable
    def get(self, name: str) -> Command | None
    def all(self) -> list[Command]  # Returns only non-hidden commands
    def help_text(self) -> str  # Formats as "  /name  description"
```

**Registration Pattern:**
Commands are registered via a loop in `_build_commands()` (lines 1560-1602) where each tuple contains `(name, handler_fn, description)`:

```python
reg.register(name, description=desc)(fn)
```

This creates a `Command` object with the async handler function and stores it in `_cmds` dict keyed by name.

minima = "minima_harness.tui.cli:main"
minima-harness = "minima_harness.tui.cli:main"

The CLI entry point handles env file loading, config parsing, provider registration, and then either launches the TUI app or runs non-interactive modes (--print, --mode json).

---

### 2. Terminal Output Library

**Framework:** **Textual 3.0+** (a modern Python TUI framework) with **Rich** for rendering text styles

**Key imports:**
- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/app.py` (lines 11-17):
  ```python

  from textual.app import App, ComposeResult

- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/welcome.py` (lines 6-7):
  ```python
  from rich.console import Group
  from rich.text import Text
  ```

**Why Textual?** The `pyproject.toml` specifies `textual>=3.0` for TUI extra support (line 64), and `textual>=3.0` specifically adds "app-wide text selection + copy_to_clipboard" so in-app drag-select and Ctrl+C copy work.

### 3. Welcome Banner & Startup Screen

**File:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/welcome.py` (lines 56-83)

This contains the complete welcome screen rendered on startup:

```python
def render_welcome(app: HarnessApp) -> Group:
    """The centered launch splash: MINIMA CLI banner + workflow strap + one onboarding hint."""
    t = get_theme(current_theme())
    accent, muted = t["accent"], t["muted"]
    
    banner = Text(BANNER, style=f"bold {accent}", justify="center")
    subtitle = Text("CLI · cost-aware model routing", style=muted, justify="center")
    strap = Text(DIAGRAM, style=muted, justify="center")
    parts = [banner, Text(""), subtitle, Text(""), strap]
    
    # Setup nudge if no provider keys are found
    if _needs_setup():
        parts.append(Text("no API keys found — run  minima config  to add them", ...))
    
    parts.append(Text("type a prompt, or / for commands", style=muted, justify="center"))
    parts.append(Text(selection_hint(getattr(app, "_mouse_enabled", True)), ...))
    return Group(*parts)
```

**Welcome screen components:**
- ASCII banner: "MINIMA" (51 cols wide, 6 rows, ANSI-Shadow style) — `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/welcome.py` lines 37-49
- Subtitle: "CLI · cost-aware model routing"
- Workflow strap: "recommend → run → judge → feedback → memory"
- Onboarding hints (setup nudge, prompt hint, mouse hint)

**Mounted by:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/app.py` line 382 in `on_mount()`:
```python
self.run_worker(self._show_welcome(), exclusive=True)
```

The welcome screen is mounted in `_show_welcome()` (lines 406-416) and dismissed on the first user prompt (line 739).

---

### 4. Interactive Prompt Loop Structure

**File:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/app.py`

**Layout (compose method, lines 348-355):**
```python
def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
    yield Header()
    yield Static(id="banner")      # Warning/notice banners

    yield OptionList(id="cmd-popup")  # / command autocomplete
    yield Editor()                 # Prompt input area
    yield StatusBar(id="status")   # Real-time metrics
    yield TextualFooter()          # Key help footer
```

   - Line 17: `render_config_banner()` — config/auth problems
   - Line 25: `render_model_error_banner()` — provider call failures
   - Line 34: `render_notice()` — general warnings/context alerts

3. **Footer rendering** — `minima_harness.tui.widgets/footer.py`:
   - Line 9-73: `render_footer()` — single canonical status surface with model/cost/metrics

4. **Status line rendering** — `minima_harness.tui.widgets/status.py`:
   - Real-time status during turn execution

5. **ChatLog message rendering** — `minima_harness.tui.widgets/messages.py`:
   - Lines 127-150: Helper methods to add messages (user, assistant, thinking, tool, system, error)

---

### Ideal Location for a "Tip" Feature

Based on the architecture, a tip would fit naturally:

1. **Between-turn location (BEST):**

   - After metrics are shown but before user can submit next prompt

3. **Command-triggered location:**
   - A `/tip` command that shows a random or rotated tip
   - Registered like other commands in the CommandRegistry

The **turn finalization point (after line 829)** is ideal because:
- It's after the assistant responds but before the editor refocuses
- Tips won't interrupt streaming or async operations
- Consistent timing for rotation logic
- Already has infrastructure for adding system messages via `chatlog.add_system()`</result>

## Report: Minima Harness Testing, Execution, and Dependencies

### 1. HOW TESTS ARE RUN

**Test Command:**
- Primary: `make test` → `uv run pytest -m "not live and not eval" -q`
- Location: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/Makefile` (line 9-10)

**Pytest Configuration:**
- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/pyproject.toml` (lines 85-92):
  - `testpaths = ["tests"]`
  - `pythonpath = ["."]`
  - `asyncio_mode = "auto"`
  - Markers defined:
    - `live`: requires running Mubit runtime (`make run-mubit`)
    - `eval`: slow offline evaluation (RouterBench)

**Test Directory Structure:**
```
tests/
  __init__.py
  conftest.py                    # Pytest fixtures (FakeMemory, TestClient)
  factories.py                   # Test doubles (FakeMemory)
  unit/                          # 30+ unit test files
  harness/                       # Agent harness tests
    test_minima_e2e.py          # Full E2E loop tests
    test_phase_*.py              # Multi-phase execution tests
    test_*.py                    # Provider, routing, cache, etc
    tools/                       # Tool-specific tests (bash, edit, grep, find, etc)
    tui/                         # Text UI (Textual) tests (30+ files)
      test_app_pilot.py
      test_run_modes.py
      test_welcome.py
      test_*.py
  integration/
  live/
  eval/
```

**Other test commands:**
- `make live` → `uv run pytest -m live -q` (requires running Mubit at 3000)
- `make eval` → `uv run pytest -m eval -q` (offline RouterBench evaluation)
- `make harness-test` → `uv run --env-file .env.harness pytest tests/harness -m live -v`

---

### 2. TESTING PATTERNS FOR CLI/REPL/UI OUTPUT

The project uses **three primary testing approaches:**

#### A. **Capsys (pytest stdout/stderr capture)**
Used in run_print/run_json modes and simple output tests:

- **File:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/tests/harness/tui/test_run_modes.py`
- **Pattern:**
  ```python
  def test_run_print_reports_provider_error(capsys):
      # ... setup ...
      rc = asyncio.run(run_print(agent, "hi"))
      captured = capsys.readouterr()
      assert rc == 1
      assert captured.err.strip()
      assert captured.out.strip() == ""
  
  def test_run_print_prints_answer_on_success(capsys):
      # ... setup ...
      rc = asyncio.run(run_print(agent, "hi"))
      captured = capsys.readouterr()
      assert "the answer" in captured.out
  ```

#### B. **EventBridge + In-Memory State Capture**
Direct capture of streamed assistant messages and tool execution:

- **File:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/bridge.py` (lines 24-100)
- **Pattern (from test_app_pilot.py, lines 22-70):**
  ```python
  async def test_app_streams_a_turn_through_minima():
      tui = HarnessApp(cfg, session=SessionStore.in_memory(), agent=agent)
      async with tui.run_test() as pilot:
          await tui.run_turn("what is the answer")
          await pilot.pause()
          
          # Direct state capture from bridge
          assert tui.bridge.assistant_text == "the answer is 42"
          assert tui.bridge.finished is True
          
          # Query rendered widgets
          from minima_harness.tui.widgets.messages import MessageBubble
          bubbles = tui.query_one(ChatLog).query(MessageBubble)
          assert any(b.buffer == "the answer is 42" for b in bubbles)
  ```
  
  The `EventBridge` dataclass tracks:
  - `assistant_text: str` - accumulated streamed text
  - `tools: list[dict]` - tool execution records
  - `turns: int` - turn counter
  - `finished: bool` - completion state
  - `error: str | None` - error capture

#### C. **Rich Console Recording (for text rendering)**

#### A. **Minima API Server (FastAPI)**

**Main Service:**
- Framework: FastAPI + uvicorn
- File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima/main.py`

```

**Environment Variables:**

**Endpoints:**

- `POST /v1/feedback` - Outcome reporting

**Lifespan & Services (main.py, lines 36-75):**
- Startup: Builds catalog store, record stores, propensity backend, decision log, reasoner, lane counter
- Background: `refresh_loop()` for catalog refresh (configurable `MINIMA_CATALOG_REFRESH_SECONDS`, default 21600s = 6h)

**Usage:**

## Report: Minima Harness Testing, Execution, and Dependencies

### 1. HOW TESTS ARE RUN

**Test Command:**
- Primary: `make test` → `uv run pytest -m "not live and not eval" -q`
- Location: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/Makefile` (line 9-10)

**Pytest Configuration:**
- `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/pyproject.toml` (lines 85-92):
  - `testpaths = ["tests"]`
  - `pythonpath = ["."]`
  - `asyncio_mode = "auto"`
  - Markers defined:
    - `live`: requires running Mubit runtime (`make run-mubit`)
    - `eval`: slow offline evaluation (RouterBench)

**Test Directory Structure:**
```
tests/
  __init__.py
  conftest.py                    # Pytest fixtures (FakeMemory, TestClient)
  factories.py                   # Test doubles (FakeMemory)
  unit/                          # 30+ unit test files
  harness/                       # Agent harness tests
    test_minima_e2e.py          # Full E2E loop tests
    test_phase_*.py              # Multi-phase execution tests
    test_*.py                    # Provider, routing, cache, etc
    tools/                       # Tool-specific tests (bash, edit, grep, find, etc)
    tui/                         # Text UI (Textual) tests (30+ files)
      test_app_pilot.py
      test_run_modes.py
      test_welcome.py
      test_*.py
  integration/
  live/
  eval/
```

**Other test commands:**
- `make live` → `uv run pytest -m live -q` (requires running Mubit at 3000)
- `make eval` → `uv run pytest -m eval -q` (offline RouterBench evaluation)
- `make harness-test` → `uv run --env-file .env.harness pytest tests/harness -m live -v`

---

### 2. TESTING PATTERNS FOR CLI/REPL/UI OUTPUT

The project uses **three primary testing approaches:**

#### A. **Capsys (pytest stdout/stderr capture)**
Used in run_print/run_json modes and simple output tests:

- **File:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/tests/harness/tui/test_run_modes.py`
- **Pattern:**
  ```python
  def test_run_print_reports_provider_error(capsys):
      # ... setup ...
      rc = asyncio.run(run_print(agent, "hi"))
      captured = capsys.readouterr()
      assert rc == 1
      assert captured.err.strip()
      assert captured.out.strip() == ""
  
  def test_run_print_prints_answer_on_success(capsys):
      # ... setup ...
      rc = asyncio.run(run_print(agent, "hi"))
      captured = capsys.readouterr()
      assert "the answer" in captured.out
  ```

#### B. **EventBridge + In-Memory State Capture**
Direct capture of streamed assistant messages and tool execution:

- **File:** `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/bridge.py` (lines 24-100)
- **Pattern (from test_app_pilot.py, lines 22-70):**
  ```python
  async def test_app_streams_a_turn_through_minima():
      tui = HarnessApp(cfg, session=SessionStore.in_memory(), agent=agent)
      async with tui.run_test() as pilot:
          await tui.run_turn("what is the answer")
          await pilot.pause()
          
          # Direct state capture from bridge
          assert tui.bridge.assistant_text == "the answer is 42"
          assert tui.bridge.finished is True
          
          # Query rendered widgets
          from minima_harness.tui.widgets.messages import MessageBubble
          bubbles = tui.query_one(ChatLog).query(MessageBubble)
          assert any(b.buffer == "the answer is 42" for b in bubbles)
  ```
  
  The `EventBridge` dataclass tracks:
  - `assistant_text: str` - accumulated streamed text
  - `tools: list[dict]` - tool execution records
  - `turns: int` - turn counter
  - `finished: bool` - completion state
  - `error: str | None` - error capture

#### C. **Rich Console Recording (for text rendering)**
Used in welcome screen and card rendering tests:

- **Fireworks**: `FIREWORKS_API_KEY` → `https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1`
- **DeepInfra**: `DEEPINFRA_TOKEN` / `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` → `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai`
- **Cerebras**: `CEREBRAS_API_KEY` → `https://api.cerebras.ai/v1`
- **Hyperbolic**: `HYPERBOLIC_API_KEY` → `https://api.hyperbolic.xyz/v1`
- **Novita**: `NOVITA_API_KEY` → `https://api.novita.ai/openai`

(File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/ai/provider_catalog.py`, lines 60-125)

---

#### B. **Escalation Reasoner (Optional, Minima's Internal LLM)**

When evidence is thin, Minima can escalate to a cheap LLM for ranking assistance:

- Configuration:
  - `MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER`: `none` | `anthropic` | `gemini`
  - `MINIMA_REASONER_MODEL`: default per provider
  - Timeout: `MINIMA_REASONER_TIMEOUT_MS=15000` (15 seconds)
  
- File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima/llm/registry.py`

- When enabled, calls the configured provider's API (same keys as above)

---

#### C. **External Data Sources (Catalog Refresh)**

Minima fetches model pricing from public sources:

- **LiteLLM prices**: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/model_prices_and_context_window.json`
  - Env: `MINIMA_LITELLM_PRICES_URL`
  - Default refresh: every `MINIMA_CATALOG_REFRESH_SECONDS=21600` (6 hours)

- **OpenRouter catalog**: `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models`
  - Env: `MINIMA_OPENROUTER_MODELS_URL`
  - Requires: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` (optional, if gating by key)

(File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima/config.py`, lines 153-160)

---

#### D. **Example Warmup Demo**

The demo mode (`examples/harness_warmup.py`) can run **entirely offline**:

- File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/examples/harness_warmup.py` (lines 1-17)
- Default: Uses in-process FakeMemory (no Mubit) + fake provider (no API calls)
- Command: `uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py` (no keys needed)

To run **live** (real Minima + real providers):
- Command: `uv run python examples/harness_warmup.py --live`
- Requirements:
  - Running Minima: `make run` on `:8080`
  - Running Mubit: `make run-mubit` on `:3000`
  - Provider keys: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, etc.

---

### 5. CONFIG LOADING & API KEYS REQUIRED

#### A. **Configuration Sources (Precedence Order)**

1. **Environment variables** (highest)
   - Standard `ENV_VAR=value` set in shell
   
2. **`.env.harness` file** (for harness CLI)
   - Location: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/.env.harness` (gitignored)
   - Loaded via: `uv run --env-file .env.harness`
   
3. **`.env` file** (for both API and harness)
   - Location: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/.env` (gitignored)
   - Auto-loaded by Pydantic-settings for `minima.config.Settings`
   
4. **OS keyring** (for harness TUI only)
   - File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/config_store.py`
   - Fallback: `~/.minima-harness/config.env` (0600 permissions)
   
5. **Environment defaults**
   - Hardcoded in `Settings` dataclass

(File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima/config.py`, lines 14-20)

#### B. **Required API Keys at Runtime**

**API Server (`minima`):**
- `MUBIT_API_KEY` (REQUIRED) — Auth for Mubit memory backend

**Harness CLI/TUI (`minima` / `minima-harness`):**
- `MUBIT_API_KEY` (REQUIRED if routing enabled) — Auth for Mubit memory backend
- At least one of:
  - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` OR `ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN`
  - `GEMINI_API_KEY` OR `GOOGLE_API_KEY` OR `GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY`
  - `OPENAI_API_KEY`
  - Other provider keys (xAI, DeepSeek, Mistral, etc.)

**Optional Keys (for features):**
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or `GEMINI_API_KEY` — If using reasoner escalation
- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` — If accessing OpenRouter's model catalog

(File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/config_cli.py`, lines 65-73)

#### C. **Configuration Setup Commands**

```bash
# Interactive guided setup
minima config

# Non-interactive
minima config set MUBIT_API_KEY <key>
minima config set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY <key>
minima config doctor   # Verify all keys are set and Minima/Mubit are reachable

# Show config
minima config list
minima config get MUBIT_API_KEY
```

(File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/src/minima_harness/tui/config_cli.py`, lines 24-100)

#### D. **Test Fixtures & Hermetic Setup**

Tests override config to stay offline:

- File: `/Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima/tests/conftest.py` (lines 17-33)
- Default fixture setup:
  ```python

  def _hermetic_offline(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:

      monkeypatch.setenv("MINIMA_DURABLE_FASTPATH", "off")
  
  @pytest.fixture
  def settings() -> Settings:
      return Settings(mubit_api_key="test-key", minima_reflect_every_n=3)
  
  @pytest.fixture
  def fake_memory() -> FakeMemory:
      return FakeMemory()  # In-process, no Mubit
  ```

---

### Summary Table: Ports & Services

| Service | Port | Type | Required | Startup |
|---------|------|------|----------|---------|
| Minima API | 8080 | HTTP (FastAPI) | For harness | `make run` |
| Mubit Memory | 3000 | gRPC/HTTP | Yes (runtime) | `make run-mubit` (separate repo) |
| PostgreSQL | 5432 | SQL | Optional (durable store) | External |
| Redis | 6379 | Cache | Optional (RecStore backend) | External |
| Anthropic API | 443 | HTTPS | If using Claude | Public SaaS |
| Google Gemini API | 443 | HTTPS | If using Gemini | Public SaaS |
| OpenAI API | 443 | HTTPS | If using GPT | Public SaaS |
| OpenRouter API | 443 | HTTPS | If using aggregator | Public SaaS |
| Ollama | 11434 | HTTP | Optional (local LLM) | User-started |
| LM Studio | 1234 | HTTP | Optional (local LLM) | User-started |

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from rich.console import Group
from rich.text import Text

from minima_harness.tui.theme import current_theme, get_theme

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from minima_harness.tui.app import HarnessApp


def selection_hint(mouse_on: bool) -> str:
    """One-line guidance for selecting/copying text given the current mouse mode.

    With mouse capture ON the wheel scrolls but the terminal's own click-drag selection is
    suppressed; you select in-app instead (drag + Ctrl+C). macOS Terminal.app can't do in-app
    drag-select (it doesn't report mouse motion), so capture there only blocks native selection —
    `/mouse off` is the way to select+copy.
    """
    if not mouse_on:
        return "native mouse select & copy · scroll with PageUp/PageDown · /mouse to toggle"
    if os.environ.get("TERM_PROGRAM") == "Apple_Terminal":
        return "Terminal.app can't drag-select while scrolling — /mouse off to select & copy"
    return "scroll: wheel/PgUp · select+copy: drag then Ctrl+C · /mouse off for native selection"

def _needs_setup() -> bool:
    """No configured provider key (across the whole provider catalog) → first-run nudge."""
    from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import configured_providers

    return not configured_providers()

# ANSI-Shadow-style block glyphs (6 rows). Built programmatically and joined row-wise so the
# columns always line up — never hand-concatenate ASCII art. Each letter's rows are equal width.
_GLYPHS: dict[str, list[str]] = {
    "M": ["███╗   ███╗", "████╗ ████║", "██╔████╔██║", "██║╚██╔╝██║", "██║ ╚═╝ ██║", "╚═╝     ╚═╝"],
    "I": ["██╗", "██║", "██║", "██║", "██║", "╚═╝"],
    "N": ["███╗   ██╗", "████╗  ██║", "██╔██╗ ██║", "██║╚██╗██║", "██║ ╚████║", "╚═╝  ╚═══╝"],
    "A": [" █████╗ ", "██╔══██╗", "███████║", "██╔══██║", "██║  ██║", "╚═╝  ╚═╝"],
}


def _ascii_banner(word: str) -> str:
    return "\n".join(" ".join(_GLYPHS[ch][row] for ch in word) for row in range(6))


BANNER = _ascii_banner("MINIMA")  # ~51 cols wide; the hero of the launch splash

# A one-line workflow strap. Live state lives in the footer (the single status surface), not
# here — the splash is pure onboarding. Auto-collapses on the first prompt; /banner toggles it.
DIAGRAM = "recommend → run → judge → feedback → memory"


def render_welcome(app: HarnessApp) -> Group:
    """The centered launch splash: MINIMA CLI banner + workflow strap + one onboarding hint.

    Carries NO live status (model/session/cost/theme — that's the footer's job) and NO
    duplicated key help.
    """
    t = get_theme(current_theme())
    accent, muted = t["accent"], t["muted"]
    # justify="center" centers each line within the (auto-width = banner-width) splash widget, so
    # the subtitle/strap/hint sit centered under the banner. The banner's 6 rows are equal width,
    # so centering them keeps the ASCII art aligned.
    banner = Text(BANNER, style=f"bold {accent}", justify="center")
    subtitle = Text("CLI · cost-aware model routing", style=muted, justify="center")
    strap = Text(DIAGRAM, style=muted, justify="center")
    parts = [banner, Text(""), subtitle, Text(""), strap]
    if _needs_setup():
        parts.append(
            Text(
                "no API keys found — run  minima config  to add them",
                style=t.get("warning", accent),
                justify="center",
            )
        )
    parts.append(Text("type a prompt, or / for commands", style=muted, justify="center"))
    parts.append(
        Text(selection_hint(getattr(app, "_mouse_enabled", True)), style=muted, justify="center")
    )
    return Group(*parts)

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
4	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
5	from typing import Any
6	
7	Handler = Callable[[Any, str], Awaitable[str | None]]
8	
9	
10	@dataclass(slots=True)
11	class Command:
12	    name: str
13	    handler: Handler
14	    description: str = ""
15	    hidden: bool = False  # dispatchable via get(), but omitted from listings (aliases)
16	
17	
18	@dataclass(slots=True)
19	class CommandRegistry:
20	    _cmds: dict[str, Command] = field(default_factory=dict)
21	
22	    def register(
23	        self, name: str, *, description: str = "", hidden: bool = False
24	    ) -> Callable[[Handler], Handler]:
25	        def deco(fn: Handler) -> Handler:
26	            self._cmds[name] = Command(
27	                name=name, handler=fn, description=description, hidden=hidden
28	            )
29	            return fn
30	
31	        return deco
32	
33	    def get(self, name: str) -> Command | None:
34	        return self._cmds.get(name)
35	
36	    def add_command(self, cmd: Command) -> None:
37	        self._cmds[cmd.name] = cmd
38	
39	    def remove_command(self, name: str) -> None:
40	        self._cmds.pop(name, None)
41	
42	    def all(self) -> list[Command]:
43	        # Hidden aliases stay dispatchable (via get) but out of the palette/help/completion.
44	        return sorted((c for c in self._cmds.values() if not c.hidden), key=lambda c: c.name)
45	
46	    def help_text(self) -> str:
47	        width = max((len(c.name) for c in self._cmds.values()), default=4)
48	        lines = [f"  /{c.name.ljust(width)}  {c.description}".rstrip() for c in self.all()]
49	        return "Commands:\n" + "\n".join(lines)
