toolUseResult.originalFile:
from __future__ import annotations

from rich.text import Text
from textual.widgets import Static

from minima_harness.tui.theme import current_theme, get_theme

_FRAMES = "⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏"


class StatusBar(Static):
    """Bottom loader/status bar: an animated spinner while the agent runs
    (routing / thinking / working), and a static status line when idle."""

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:  # noqa: ANN002, ANN003
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        self._state = "idle"  # idle | routing | thinking | working
        self._frame = 0
        self._idle_text: Text | str = ""
        self._tip = ""  # a "💡 …" spinner tip shown beside the loader while busy (empty = none)
        self._timer = None

    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        # The spinner timer runs ONLY while the agent is busy — paused at idle so the event
        # loop (and the terminal) can truly sleep instead of waking 10x/s for a no-op tick.
        self._timer = self.set_interval(0.1, self._tick, pause=True)

    def _tick(self) -> None:
        self._frame = (self._frame + 1) % len(_FRAMES)
        self._display()

    def set_state(self, state: str) -> None:
        # Idempotent: the streaming path calls this on every token delta with the same state,
        # which would otherwise repaint the footer per token (50-100x/s) and spin fans. Only
        # (re)render and toggle the spinner timer when the state actually changes.
        if state == self._state:
            return
        self._state = state
        if self._timer is not None:
            if state == "idle":
                self._timer.pause()
            else:
                self._frame = 0
                self._timer.resume()
        self._display()

    def set_idle_text(self, text: Text | str) -> None:
        # Accept a rich Text so the footer's per-segment colours survive (don't flatten).
        self._idle_text = text
        if self._state == "idle":
            self._display()

    def set_tip(self, tip: str) -> None:
        # The tip is chosen once per turn (not per spinner tick), so the text is constant while the
        # frame animates. Repaint immediately if we're already busy so it appears mid-turn.
        self._tip = tip
        if self._state != "idle":
            self._display()

    def _display(self) -> None:
        t = get_theme(current_theme())
        if self._state == "idle":
            self.update(self._idle_text or "")
            return
        line = Text(f"{_FRAMES[self._frame]} {self._state}…", style=t["accent"])
        if self._tip:
            line.append(f"  {self._tip}", style=t["muted"])
        self.update(line)

from __future__ import annotations

from rich.console import Console

from minima_harness.tui.widgets.status import StatusBar


def _rendered(bar: StatusBar) -> str:
    # _display() pushes a renderable into the Static via .update(); pull the last value back out.
    bar._display()
    console = Console(record=True, width=80)
    console.print(bar.renderable)
    return console.export_text()


def test_tip_shows_beside_spinner_when_busy():
    bar = StatusBar()
    bar._state = "working"  # simulate a busy turn without mounting the widget/timer
    bar.set_tip("💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions")
    out = _rendered(bar)
    assert "working" in out
    assert "💡" in out and "/recall" in out


def test_tip_hidden_when_idle():
    bar = StatusBar()
    bar.set_tip("💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions")
    # state stays idle → idle text only, no tip leaking into the resting status line
    out = _rendered(bar)
    assert "💡" not in out


def test_set_state_is_idempotent():
    bar = StatusBar()
    # No timer mounted; repeated same-state calls must be a no-op and never raise.
    bar.set_state("idle")
    bar.set_state("idle")
    assert bar._state == "idle"

15	def selection_hint(mouse_on: bool) -> str:
16	    """One-line guidance for selecting/copying text given the current mouse mode.
17	
18	    With mouse capture ON the wheel scrolls but the terminal's own click-drag selection is
19	    suppressed; you select in-app instead (drag + Ctrl+C). macOS Terminal.app can't do in-app
20	    drag-select (it doesn't report mouse motion), so capture there only blocks native selection —
21	    `/mouse off` is the way to select+copy.
22	    """
23	    if not mouse_on:
24	        return "native mouse select & copy · scroll with PageUp/PageDown · /mouse to toggle"
25	    if os.environ.get("TERM_PROGRAM") == "Apple_Terminal":
26	        return "Terminal.app can't drag-select while scrolling — /mouse off to select & copy"
27	    return "scroll: wheel/PgUp · select+copy: drag then Ctrl+C · /mouse off for native selection"
28	
29	def _needs_setup() -> bool:
30	    """No configured provider key (across the whole provider catalog) → first-run nudge."""
31	    from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import configured_providers
32	
33	    return not configured_providers()
34	
35	# ANSI-Shadow-style block glyphs (6 rows). Built programmatically and joined row-wise so the
36	# columns always line up — never hand-concatenate ASCII art. Each letter's rows are equal width.
37	_GLYPHS: dict[str, list[str]] = {
38	    "M": ["███╗   ███╗", "████╗ ████║", "██╔████╔██║", "██║╚██╔╝██║", "██║ ╚═╝ ██║", "╚═╝     ╚═╝"],
39	    "I": ["██╗", "██║", "██║", "██║", "██║", "╚═╝"],
40	    "N": ["███╗   ██╗", "████╗  ██║", "██╔██╗ ██║", "██║╚██╗██║", "██║ ╚████║", "╚═╝  ╚═══╝"],
41	    "A": [" █████╗ ", "██╔══██╗", "███████║", "██╔══██║", "██║  ██║", "╚═╝  ╚═╝"],
42	}
43	
44	
45	def _ascii_banner(word: str) -> str:
46	    return "\n".join(" ".join(_GLYPHS[ch][row] for ch in word) for row in range(6))
47	
48	
49	BANNER = _ascii_banner("MINIMA")  # ~51 cols wide; the hero of the launch splash
50	
51	# A one-line workflow strap. Live state lives in the footer (the single status surface), not
52	# here — the splash is pure onboarding. Auto-collapses on the first prompt; /banner toggles it.
53	DIAGRAM = "recommend → run → judge → feedback → memory"
54	
55	
56	def render_welcome(app: HarnessApp) -> Group:
57	    """The centered launch splash: MINIMA CLI banner + workflow strap + one onboarding hint.
58	
59	    Carries NO live status (model/session/cost/theme — that's the footer's job) and NO
60	    duplicated key help.
61	    """
62	    t = get_theme(current_theme())
63	    accent, muted = t["accent"], t["muted"]
64	    # justify="center" centers each line within the (auto-width = banner-width) splash widget, so
65	    # the subtitle/strap/hint sit centered under the banner. The banner's 6 rows are equal width,
66	    # so centering them keeps the ASCII art aligned.
67	    banner = Text(BANNER, style=f"bold {accent}", justify="center")
68	    subtitle = Text("CLI · cost-aware model routing", style=muted, justify="center")
69	    strap = Text(DIAGRAM, style=muted, justify="center")
70	    parts = [banner, Text(""), subtitle, Text(""), strap]
71	    if _needs_setup():
72	        parts.append(
73	            Text(
74	                "no API keys found — run  minima config  to add them",
75	                style=t.get("warning", accent),
76	                justify="center",
77	            )
78	        )
79	    parts.append(Text("type a prompt, or / for commands", style=muted, justify="center"))
80	    parts.append(
81	        Text(selection_hint(getattr(app, "_mouse_enabled", True)), style=muted, justify="center")
82	    )
83	    return Group(*parts)

18	def _hermetic_offline(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
19	    """Keep offline tests independent of a developer's local ``.env``.
20	
21	    A populated ``.env`` (e.g. ``MINIMA_REASONER_PROVIDER=gemini`` + a key) would
22	    otherwise leak into every ``Settings()`` a test doesn't fully override, building a
23	    real reasoner and calling the provider on escalation. Env vars outrank the ``.env``
24	    file, and explicit ``Settings(...)`` kwargs still outrank these — so reasoner tests
25	    that opt in via kwargs (escalation, registry, live) keep working.
26	
27	    The model is cleared too: a live test may pin a *provider* without pinning a model,
28	    and must not inherit a foreign model id from ``.env`` (e.g. an Anthropic test ending
29	    up pointed at a Gemini model) — clearing it falls back to each provider's default.

from __future__ import annotations

from rich.text import Text
from textual.widgets import Static

from minima_harness.tui.theme import current_theme, get_theme

_FRAMES = "⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏"


class StatusBar(Static):
    """Bottom loader/status bar: an animated spinner while the agent runs
    (routing / thinking / working), and a static status line when idle."""

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:  # noqa: ANN002, ANN003
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        self._state = "idle"  # idle | routing | thinking | working
        self._frame = 0
        self._idle_text: Text | str = ""
        self._tip = ""  # a "💡 …" spinner tip shown beside the loader while busy (empty = none)
        self._timer = None

    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        # The spinner timer runs ONLY while the agent is busy — paused at idle so the event
        # loop (and the terminal) can truly sleep instead of waking 10x/s for a no-op tick.
        self._timer = self.set_interval(0.1, self._tick, pause=True)

    def _tick(self) -> None:
        self._frame = (self._frame + 1) % len(_FRAMES)
        self._display()

    def set_state(self, state: str) -> None:
        # Idempotent: the streaming path calls this on every token delta with the same state,
        # which would otherwise repaint the footer per token (50-100x/s) and spin fans. Only
        # (re)render and toggle the spinner timer when the state actually changes.
        if state == self._state:
            return
        self._state = state
        if self._timer is not None:
            if state == "idle":
                self._timer.pause()
            else:
                self._frame = 0
                self._timer.resume()
        self._display()

    def set_idle_text(self, text: Text | str) -> None:
        # Accept a rich Text so the footer's per-segment colours survive (don't flatten).
        self._idle_text = text
        if self._state == "idle":
            self._display()

    def set_tip(self, tip: str) -> None:
        # The tip is chosen once per turn (not per spinner tick), so the text is constant while the
        # frame animates. Repaint immediately if we're already busy so it appears mid-turn.
        self._tip = tip
        if self._state != "idle":
            self._display()

    def _render(self) -> Text | str:
        """The renderable for the current state — pure, so it's testable without a mounted app."""
        if self._state == "idle":
            return self._idle_text or ""
        t = get_theme(current_theme())
        line = Text(f"{_FRAMES[self._frame]} {self._state}…", style=t["accent"])
        if self._tip:
            line.append(f"  {self._tip}", style=t["muted"])
        return line

    def _display(self) -> None:
        self.update(self._render())

input.new_string:
def _status_renderable(self) -> Text | str:
        """The renderable for the current state — pure, so it's testable without a mounted app.

        NB: must NOT be named ``_render`` — that's a Textual ``Widget`` internal; overriding it
        corrupts the render pipeline (a raw ``Text`` reaches ``Visual.to_strips``).
        """
        if self._state == "idle":
            return self._idle_text or ""
        t = get_theme(current_theme())
        line = Text(f"{_FRAMES[self._frame]} {self._state}…", style=t["accent"])
        if self._tip:
            line.append(f"  {self._tip}", style=t["muted"])
        return line

    def _display(self) -> None:
        self.update(self._status_renderable())

from __future__ import annotations

from rich.console import Console

from minima_harness.tui.widgets.status import StatusBar


def _rendered(bar: StatusBar) -> str:
    # _render() is the pure renderable builder (no mounted app needed).
    console = Console(record=True, width=80)
    console.print(bar._render())
    return console.export_text()


def test_tip_shows_beside_spinner_when_busy():
    bar = StatusBar()
    bar._state = "working"  # simulate a busy turn without mounting the widget/timer
    bar.set_tip("💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions")
    out = _rendered(bar)
    assert "working" in out
    assert "💡" in out and "/recall" in out


def test_tip_hidden_when_idle():
    bar = StatusBar()
    bar.set_tip("💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions")
    # state stays idle → idle text only, no tip leaking into the resting status line
    out = _rendered(bar)
    assert "💡" not in out


def test_set_state_is_idempotent():
    bar = StatusBar()
    # No timer mounted; repeated same-state calls must be a no-op and never raise.
    bar.set_state("idle")
    bar.set_state("idle")
    assert bar._state == "idle"

+    # _status_renderable() is the pure renderable builder (no mounted app needed).

from __future__ import annotations

from rich.console import Console

from minima_harness.tui.widgets.status import StatusBar


def _rendered(bar: StatusBar) -> str:
    # _status_renderable() is the pure renderable builder (no mounted app needed).
    console = Console(record=True, width=80)
    console.print(bar._status_renderable())
    return console.export_text()


def test_tip_shows_beside_spinner_when_busy():
    bar = StatusBar()
    bar._state = "working"  # simulate a busy turn without mounting the widget/timer
    bar.set_tip("💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions")
    out = _rendered(bar)
    assert "working" in out
    assert "💡" in out and "/recall" in out


def test_tip_hidden_when_idle():
    bar = StatusBar()
    bar.set_tip("💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions")
    # state stays idle → idle text only, no tip leaking into the resting status line
    out = _rendered(bar)
    assert "💡" not in out


def test_set_state_is_idempotent():
    bar = StatusBar()
    # No timer mounted; repeated same-state calls must be a no-op and never raise.
    bar.set_state("idle")
    bar.set_state("idle")
    assert bar._state == "idle"

lines: -    bar._state = "working"  # simulate a busy turn without mounting the widget/timer
-    bar.set_tip("💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions")
+    # Set state/tip directly: set_tip()/set_state() would call _display()→update(), which needs a
+    # mounted app. _status_renderable() is the pure path we assert on.
+    bar._tip = "💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions"
assert "💡" in out and "/recall" in out

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan: "Tip …" passive command discovery for the Minima harness

## Context

Minima's TUI exposes ~39 `/` slash commands, several of which are genuinely distinctive
(`/recall`, `/escalate`, `/confirm`, `/cache`, `/optimize`, `/ledger`, `/judge`, `/edits`,
`/tree`+`/fork`+`/clone`, `/optimize`, `/prompt`). New users rarely discover them — the only
existing hints are the one-line onboarding nudges on the welcome splash (`welcome.py`).

The goal: let users **passively** learn these commands. Per the user's direction, we mirror how
**Claude Code** does it — **spinner tips**: short rotating hints shown next to the working
spinner (Claude Code merges built-in tips with custom ones via a `tips` array + `excludeDefault`).
We also keep Claude Code's "getting-started hint on the welcome screen" idea, and add a
Codex-style on-demand `/tip` command. Rotation uses a **persisted cycle** so every launch shows a
new tip in order.

Sources reviewed:
- Claude Code spinner tips / welcome screen: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart , https://support.claude.com/en/articles/14554000-claude-code-power-user-tips
- Codex CLI slash-command discovery: https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/features

## Where tips will surface (all three, mirroring Claude Code + Codex)

1. **Spinner tip (primary — Claude Code style).** While the agent is busy
   (`StatusBar` state `routing`/`thinking`/`working`), render the spinner line as
   `⠹ working…  💡 /recall pulls lessons from past sessions, even across projects`.
   A new tip is chosen once per turn (when state leaves `idle`), so it does **not** flicker on
   every 0.1s spinner tick or every streamed token.
2. **Welcome splash tip (onboarding).** One rotating `💡 Tip · …` line appended to the launch
   splash (`render_welcome`), cycling per launch.
3. **`/tip` command (Codex style, on demand).** `/tip` prints the next tip into the transcript and
   cycles; `/tip off` / `/tip on` toggle the spinner tips (mirrors Claude Code's `excludeDefault`).

## Rotation: persisted cycle

A tiny JSON state file `~/.minima-harness/tips_state.json` holds `{"index": N}`. A module-level
`advance() -> int` reads, increments (mod len), writes, and returns the index — wrapped in
try/except so a read-only HOME never crashes the app (falls back to in-memory). `GLOBAL_DIR`
already exists for `config.env` (`config_store.py:22`), so reuse that directory.

- Welcome calls `advance()` once at app construction → stored on `self._tip_index`.
- Each turn start calls `advance()` → sets the spinner's current tip.
- `/tip` calls `advance()` → prints next.

## Files to create / modify

### New: `src/minima_harness/tui/tips.py`
- `TIPS: tuple[str, ...]` — curated, each highlighting one distinctive command, e.g.:
  - `"/recall pulls lessons from past sessions, even across projects"`
  - `"/escalate auto-retries on a stronger model when answer quality looks thin"`
  - `"/confirm shows the routing trade-off panel before each turn so you pick the model"`
  - `"/model auto lets Minima cost-route; /model <id> pins one"`
  - `"/cache reuses a near-duplicate answer for $0 — toggle the semantic cache"`
  - `"/optimize trims your system prompt via Mubit to save tokens"`
  - `"/ledger set <goal> tracks spend against a budget across the whole goal"`
  - `"/judge toggles LLM quality judging of each answer"`
  - `"/edits forces a diff review before any file write"`
  - `"/tree, /fork and /clone branch and revisit your session history"`
  - `"/thoughts streams the model's reasoning live"`
  - `"/cost opens the meter: estimated vs. actual spend per turn"`
  - `"/compact summarizes older context when the window fills up"`
  - `"/prompt inspects the layered Mubit + local system prompt"`
  - `"/stats shows analytics across your last 10 sessions"`
- `format_tip(body: str) -> str` → `f"💡 {body}"` (welcome uses `💡 Tip · {body}`).
- `pick(index: int) -> str` → `TIPS[index % len(TIPS)]`.
- `advance() -> int` → persisted-cycle increment described above (try/except, in-memory fallback).
- Keep it import-light (no Textual import) so it unit-tests in isolation, like `welcome.selection_hint`.

### Modify: `src/minima_harness/tui/welcome.py`
- In `render_welcome`, after the existing `"type a prompt, or / for commands"` hint, append
  `Text(format_tip("Tip · " + pick(getattr(app, "_tip_index", 0))), style=muted, justify="center")`.

### Modify: `src/minima_harness/tui/widgets/status.py`
- Add `self._tip: str = ""` and `def set_tip(self, tip: str)`.
- In `_display()`, for non-idle states render
  `Text(f"{_FRAMES[frame]} {state}…", accent)` then append `"  " + self._tip` (muted style) when
  `self._tip` is set. Spinner tip text stays constant across ticks (only the frame animates).

### Modify: `src/minima_harness/tui/app.py`
- `__init__`: `self._tip_index = tips.advance()` (drives the welcome splash) and
  `self._tips_enabled = True`.
- `run_turn` (near `self._set_state("routing")`, ~line 751): if `self._tips_enabled`, pick a fresh
  tip via `tips.advance()` and call `self.query_one(StatusBar).set_tip(tips.format_tip(pick(...)))`;
  otherwise `set_tip("")`. Clear the tip in `_after_turn` / on return to idle so the idle footer is
  unchanged (idle state already renders `_idle_text`, so the tip simply isn't shown at idle).
- Register a `_tip` handler in `_build_commands` (the list at ~line 1560):
  `("tip", _tip, "show a command tip (/tip off to silence spinner tips)")`.
  `_tip`: `off`/`on` toggles `self._tips_enabled` (+ system confirmation line); no arg →
  `await chatlog.add_system(tips.format_tip(pick(tips.advance())))`.

## Tests (hermetic, offline — no servers, no ports, no API keys)

Follow the existing conventions in `tests/harness/tui/`:
- **`tests/harness/tui/test_tips.py`** (new):
  - `pick` wraps modulo `len(TIPS)`; every tip contains a `/command` and is non-empty.
  - `advance()` increments and persists: monkeypatch the state path to `tmp_path`, call twice,
    assert returned indices differ and the JSON file holds the latest index. Read-only/unwritable
    path falls back without raising.
  - `format_tip` prefixes `💡`.
- **Extend `tests/harness/tui/test_welcome.py`**: assert the rendered splash contains `💡` and a
  `/` command (reuse the existing `_render_text` Console-record helper).
- **`tests/harness/tui/test_status_tip.py`** (new): construct `StatusBar`, `set_state("working")`,
  `set_tip("💡 /recall …")`, render via `Console(record=True)` and assert the tip text shows for a
  busy state and is absent at idle; assert `set_state` stays idempotent (no exception on repeat).
- **`/tip` command test** (in a new or existing app-pilot test): drive `app.run_turn`-free path —
  dispatch `/tip` via `app._dispatch_command("tip", "")` inside `app.run_test()` and assert a
  system bubble with `💡` is added; `/tip off` sets `app._tips_enabled is False`.

Run: `make test` (i.e. `uv run pytest -m "not live and not eval" -q`), or target just the new files
with `uv run pytest tests/harness/tui/test_tips.py tests/harness/tui/test_status_tip.py tests/harness/tui/test_welcome.py -q`.

## Verification — how to start Minima and see the change

The harness TUI itself **binds no network port**. It can run fully offline:

```bash
cd /Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima
uv run minima --allow-offline        # launches the Textual TUI
```

- On launch you'll see the MINIMA splash with a new `💡 Tip · …` line; relaunch to see the next tip
  in the cycle (persisted in `~/.minima-harness/tips_state.json`).
- Type any prompt: while it's "routing…/working…", the status line shows `💡 /<command> …` next to
  the spinner (needs a provider key to actually run a turn; offline still shows the spinner tip
  briefly before the offline fallback). To run real turns, set one provider key
  (e.g. `export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=…`) or run `uv run minima config`.
- Type `/tip` to cycle on demand; `/tip off` to silence spinner tips, `/tip on` to re-enable.

## ⚠️ Services & ports (for the parallel Claude session)

- **My implementation + tests use NO servers and bind NO ports.** Tests are hermetic/offline
  (`conftest.py` forces offline) and need no API keys.
- The TUI (`uv run minima`) binds nothing itself. Only if you run it **live** does it reach:
  - **Mubit** on `:3000` (`http://127.0.0.1:3000`) — only if you start `make run-mubit`.
  - **Minima API** on `:8080` (`make run`) — optional; `.env.example` suggests `8088` to avoid the
    Mubit collision on 8080.
- I will **not** run `make run`, `make run-mubit`, or bind 3000/8080/8088. If the other session
  needs those ports, there's no conflict from this work. I'll only run `uv run pytest` and
  `uv run minima --allow-offline` (no listeners).

lines: -uv run minima --allow-offline        # launches the Textual TUI
+uv run minima --offline        # launches the Textual TUI (flag is --offline)

content.text:
The feature is implemented, tested, and verified. Here's the final report.

## ✅ Done: "Tip …" passive command discovery for Minima

I built the feature exactly like **Claude Code's spinner tips** (rotating hints next to the working spinner, plus a welcome-screen hint), with a **Codex-style `/tip` command**, and a **persisted rotation cycle**.

### What I built

**New file `src/minima_harness/tui/tips.py`** — 15 curated tips, each spotlighting a *distinctive* Minima command (the ones a new user would never find): `/recall`, `/escalate`, `/confirm`, `/model auto`, `/cache`, `/optimize`, `/ledger`, `/judge`, `/edits`, `/tree`+`/fork`+`/clone`, `/thoughts`, `/cost`, `/compact`, `/prompt`, `/stats`. Rotation index persists in `~/.minima-harness/tips_state.json` (best-effort; falls back to in-memory if HOME is read-only).

**Three surfaces** (as previewed above):
1. **Welcome splash** — one rotating `💡 Tip · …` line; a new tip each launch (`welcome.py`).
2. **Spinner tip** — while busy, the status bar shows `⠋ working…  💡 /model auto …`, chosen once per turn so it never flickers (`widgets/status.py` + `app.py:run_turn`).
3. **`/tip` command** — prints the next tip on demand; `/tip off` / `/tip on` silence/restore spinner tips (registered in `app.py`).

### A real bug I hit and fixed
My first status-bar refactor named a helper `_render()` — which **collides with a Textual `Widget` internal** and corrupted the render pipeline (crashed the app on mount). I bisected it against a clean baseline, confirmed the cause, and renamed it to `_status_renderable()` with a comment warning future readers.

### Tests (all hermetic — no network, no keys)
- `test_tips.py` — rotation/wrap/persistence + unwritable-path fallback
- `test_status_tip.py` — tip beside spinner when busy, hidden at idle, `set_state` idempotent
- `test_tip_command.py` — `/tip` prints, `/tip off`/`on` toggles, command registered
- extended `test_welcome.py` — splash shows the tip
- `tests/conftest.py` — redirects the tip state file to a temp dir so the suite never writes to your real `~/.minima-harness`

**Results:** all 208 TUI tests pass; 700 passed overall. The only failures (`test_store_backends.py`) are **pre-existing and environmental** — they need a running Redis (`:6379`) and Postgres (`:5432`); I verified they fail identically with my changes reverted.

### How to start Minima and see it
```bash
cd /Users/eldaru/Mubit/Minima/minima
uv run minima --offline      # flag is --offline
```
- Splash shows a `💡 Tip` line; relaunch to see the next tip in the cycle.
- Type `/tip` to cycle on demand; `/tip off` to silence spinner tips.
- To see the spinner tip on a real turn, set a provider key (`export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=…` or `uv run minima config`) and send a prompt.

### ⚠️ Services & ports (for your parallel Claude session)
**Nothing I ran binds a port or starts a server.** I only ran `uv run pytest` (hermetic) and `uv run minima --help` + offline render snippets. I did **not** touch:
- **`:3000`** — Mubit memory (`make run-mubit`)
- **`:8080` / `:8088`** — Minima API (`make run`)

No conflict from my side. If you run the TUI *live* yourself, it would reach Mubit `:3000` (and optionally the API), but my work doesn't.

One note: this is on the `feat/websearch` branch, which already had unrelated uncommitted changes (web-search tooling) before I started — I left those untouched. My changes are the `tips.py` / `status.py` / `welcome.py` / `app.py` / conftest + the four test files.

content: Built a passive "Tip" feature for the Minima harness, surfacing distinctive slash commands via welcome splash, spinner tips, and a /tip command. It's implemented, tested (208 TUI tests pass), and verified. Next: review or commit when you're ready. (disable recaps in /config)
